text.NRSV: These are the words that Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan—in the wilderness, on the plain opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Di-zahab.
text.Rotherham: These, are the words, which Moses spake unto all Israel, over the Jordan,—in the wilderness in the waste plain over against Suph, between Paran and Tophel, and Laban and Hazeroth and Di-zahab:
text.KJV: These be the words which Moses spake unto all Israel on this side Jordan in the wilderness, in the plain over against the Red sea, between Paran, and Tophel, and Laban, and Hazeroth, and Dizahab.
text.NABRE: I. First Address Chapter 1 Introduction. These are the words that Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan in the wilderness, in the Arabah, opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Dizahab.
text.DRB: These are the words, which Moyses spake to all Israel beyond Jordan, in the champion wilderness, against the Red sea, between Pharan and Tophel and Laban and Haseroth, where there is very much gold:

text.NRSV: (By the way of Mount Seir it takes eleven days to reach Kadesh-barnea from Horeb.)
text.Rotherham: eleven days from Horeb, by way of Mount Seir, as far as Kadesh-barnea.
text.KJV: (There are eleven days’ journey from Horeb by the way of mount Seir unto Kadesh–barnea.)
text.NABRE: It is a journey of eleven days from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea by way of the highlands of Seir.
text.DRB: eleven days from Horeb by the way of mount Seir to Cadesbarne.

text.NRSV: In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses spoke to the Israelites just as the Lord had commanded him to speak to them.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the fortieth year, in the eleventh month, on the first of the month, that Moses spake unto the sons of Israel, according to all that Yahweh had given him in charge for them;
text.KJV: And it came to pass in the fortieth year, in the eleventh month, on the first day of the month, that Moses spake unto the children of Israel, according unto all that the Lord had given him in commandment unto them;
text.NABRE: In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses spoke to the Israelites according to all that the Lord had commanded him to speak to them,
text.DRB: The fortieth year, the eleventh month, the first day of the month Moyses spake to the children of Israel all things that our Lord had commanded him to say unto them:

text.NRSV: This was after he had defeated King Sihon of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, and King Og of Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei.
text.Rotherham: after he had smitten Sihon, king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon,—and Og, king of Bashan, who dwelt in Ashtaroth, in Edrei:
text.KJV: After he had slain Sihon the king of the Amorites, which dwelt in Heshbon, and Og the king of Bashan, which dwelt at Astaroth in Edrei:
text.NABRE: after he had defeated Sihon, king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, and Og, king of Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei.
text.DRB: after that he had struck Sehon king of the Amorrheites, which dwelt in Hesebon: and Og the king of Basan which abode in Aseroth, and in Edrai,

text.NRSV: Beyond the Jordan in the land of Moab, Moses undertook to expound this law as follows:
text.Rotherham: over the Jordan, in the land of Moab, did Moses take in hand to expound this law, saying:—
text.KJV: On this side Jordan, in the land of Moab, began Moses to declare this law, saying,
text.NABRE: Beyond the Jordan, in the land of Moab, Moses undertook to explain this law:
text.DRB: beyond Jordan in the Land of Moab. And Moyses began to expound the law, and to say:

text.NRSV: The Lord our God spoke to us at Horeb, saying, “You have stayed long enough at this mountain.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh our God, spake unto us in Horeb, saying, Long enough, have ye dwelt in this mountain:
text.KJV: The Lord our God spake unto us in Horeb, saying, Ye have dwelt long enough in this mount:
text.NABRE: Departure from Horeb. The Lord, our God, said to us at Horeb: You have stayed long enough at this mountain.
text.DRB: The Lord our God spake to us in Horeb, saying: It is sufficient for you that you have stayed in this mountain:

text.NRSV: Resume your journey, and go into the hill country of the Amorites as well as into the neighboring regions—the Arabah, the hill country, the Shephelah, the Negeb, and the seacoast—the land of the Canaanites and the Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Euphrates.
text.Rotherham: Turn ye and set yourselves forward, and enter into the hill country of the Amorites, and into all the places near, in the plain, in the mountain, and in the lowland, and in the south, and in the coast of the sea,—the land of Canaan and the Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Euphrates.
text.KJV: Turn you, and take your journey, and go to the mount of the Amorites, and unto all the places nigh thereunto, in the plain, in the hills, and in the vale, and in the south, and by the sea side, to the land of the Canaanites, and unto Lebanon, unto the great river, the river Euphrates.
text.NABRE: Leave here and go to the hill country of the Amorites and to all the surrounding regions, the Arabah, the mountains, the Shephelah, the Negeb and the seacoast—the land of the Canaanites and the Lebanon as far as the Great River, the Euphrates.
text.DRB: return, and come to the mountain of the Amorrheites, and to the rest that are next to it champion and hilly and lower places against the South, and beside the shore of the sea, the Land of the Chananites, and of Libanus unto the great river Euphrates.

text.NRSV: See, I have set the land before you; go in and take possession of the land that I swore to your ancestors, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give to them and to their descendants after them.”
text.Rotherham: See! I have set before you the land,—enter and possess the land, which, Yahweh sware unto your fathers to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give unto them, and unto their seed, after them.
text.KJV: Behold, I have set the land before you: go in and possess the land which the Lord sware unto your fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, to give unto them and to their seed after them.
text.NABRE: See, I have given that land over to you. Go now and possess the land that the Lord swore to your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, to give to them and to their descendants after them.
text.DRB: Behold (quoth he) I have delivered it to you: enter in and possess it, upon the which our Lord sware to your fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, that he would give it to them, & to their seed after them.

text.NRSV: At that time I said to you, “I am unable by myself to bear you.
text.Rotherham: So I spake unto you, at that time saying, I am unable, by myself, to carry you.
text.KJV: And I spake unto you at that time, saying, I am not able to bear you myself alone:
text.NABRE: Appointment of Elders. At that time I said to you, “I am unable to carry you by myself.
text.DRB: And I said to you at that time:

text.NRSV: The Lord your God has multiplied you, so that today you are as numerous as the stars of heaven.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh your God hath multiplied you, and here ye are to-day, like the stars of the heavens for multitude.
text.KJV: The Lord your God hath multiplied you, and, behold, ye are this day as the stars of heaven for multitude.
text.NABRE: The Lord, your God, has made you numerous, and now you are as numerous as the stars of the heavens.
text.DRB: I alone cannot sustain you, because the Lord your God hath multiplied you, and you are this day as the stars of heaven, very many.

text.NRSV: May the Lord, the God of your ancestors, increase you a thousand times more and bless you, as he has promised you!
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, the God of your fathers, be adding unto you the like of you, a thousand times,—and bless you, as he hath spoken unto you!
text.KJV: (The Lord God of your fathers make you a thousand times so many more as ye are, and bless you, as he hath promised you!)
text.NABRE: May the Lord, the God of your ancestors, increase you a thousand times over, and bless you as he promised!
text.DRB: (The Lord God of your fathers add to this number many thousands, and bless you as he hath spoken.)

text.NRSV: But how can I bear the heavy burden of your disputes all by myself?
text.Rotherham: How should I carry by myself the fatigue of you and the burden of you and your controversies?
text.KJV: How can I myself alone bear your cumbrance, and your burden, and your strife?
text.NABRE: But how can I, by myself, bear the weight, the contentiousness of you?
text.DRB: I alone am not able to sustain your businesses, and the charge of you and your quarrels.

text.NRSV: Choose for each of your tribes individuals who are wise, discerning, and reputable to be your leaders.”
text.Rotherham: Set forth for you—men wise and discerning, and known to your tribes,—that I may appoint them to be heads over you.
text.KJV: Take you wise men, and understanding, and known among your tribes, and I will make them rulers over you.
text.NABRE: Provide wise, discerning, and reputable persons for each of your tribes, that I may appoint them as your leaders.”
text.DRB: Give from among you wise and skillful men, and such whose conversation is approved in your tribes, that I may appoint them your princes.

text.NRSV: You answered me, “The plan you have proposed is a good one.”
text.Rotherham: And ye answered me,—and said, Good, is the word which thou hast spoken to be done.
text.KJV: And ye answered me, and said, The thing which thou hast spoken is good for us to do.
text.NABRE: You answered me, “What you have proposed is good.”
text.DRB: Then you answered me: The thing is good which thou meanest to do.

text.NRSV: So I took the leaders of your tribes, wise and reputable individuals, and installed them as leaders over you, commanders of thousands, commanders of hundreds, commanders of fifties, commanders of tens, and officials, throughout your tribes.
text.Rotherham: So I took heads for your tribes wise men and known, and placed them as heads over you, captains of thousands, and captains of hundreds, and captains of fifties and captains of tens, and overseers for your tribes.
text.KJV: So I took the chief of your tribes, wise men, and known, and made them heads over you, captains over thousands, and captains over hundreds, and captains over fifties, and captains over tens, and officers among your tribes.
text.NABRE: So I took the leaders of your tribes, wise and reputable, and set them as leaders over you, commanders over thousands, over hundreds, over fifties and over tens, and other tribal officers.
text.DRB: And I took of your tribes men wise and noble, and appointed them Princes, tribunes, and centurions, and quinquagenarians, and deans, that might teach you all things.

text.NRSV: I charged your judges at that time: “Give the members of your community a fair hearing, and judge rightly between one person and another, whether citizen or resident alien.
text.Rotherham: And I charged your judges, at that time, saying,—Ye are to hear between your brethren and shall judge righteously, between each man and his brother and his sojourner.
text.KJV: And I charged your judges at that time, saying, Hear the causes between your brethren, and judge righteously between every man and his brother, and the stranger that is with him.
text.NABRE: I charged your judges at that time, “Listen to complaints among your relatives, and administer true justice to both parties even if one of them is a resident alien.
text.DRB: And I commanded them, saying: Hear them, and judge that which is just: whether he be the same country man, or a stranger.

text.NRSV: You must not be partial in judging: hear out the small and the great alike; you shall not be intimidated by anyone, for the judgment is God’s. Any case that is too hard for you, bring to me, and I will hear it.”
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not take note of faces in judgment—so the small as the great, shall ye hear, ye shall not shrink from the face of any man, for judgment, unto God, belongeth,—but the thing which is too hard for you, shall ye bring near unto me, and I will hear it.
text.KJV: Ye shall not respect persons in judgment; but ye shall hear the small as well as the great; ye shall not be afraid of the face of man; for the judgment is God’s: and the cause that is too hard for you, bring it unto me, and I will hear it.
text.NABRE: In rendering judgment, do not consider who a person is; give ear to the lowly and to the great alike, fearing no one, for the judgment is God’s. Any case that is too difficult for you bring to me and I will hear it.”
text.DRB: There shall be no difference of persons, so shall you hear the little as the great: neither shall you accept any man's person, because it is the judgment of God. And if any thing seem hard to you, refer it to me, and I will hear it.

text.NRSV: So I charged you at that time with all the things that you should do.
text.Rotherham: So I commanded you at that time all the things which ye should do.
text.KJV: And I commanded you at that time all the things which ye should do.
text.NABRE: Thus I charged you, at that time, with all the things you were to do.
text.DRB: And I commanded all things that you ought to do.

text.NRSV: Then, just as the Lord our God had ordered us, we set out from Horeb and went through all that great and terrible wilderness that you saw, on the way to the hill country of the Amorites, until we reached Kadesh-barnea.
text.Rotherham: And when we brake up from Horeb, we came through all that great and terrible desert which ye saw, by way of the hill-country of the Amorites, as Yahweh our God commanded us,—and we entered in as far as Kadesh-barnea.
text.KJV: And when we departed from Horeb, we went through all that great and terrible wilderness, which ye saw by the way of the mountain of the Amorites, as the Lord our God commanded us; and we came to Kadesh–barnea.
text.NABRE: The Twelve Scouts. Then we set out from Horeb and journeyed through that whole vast and fearful wilderness that you have seen, in the direction of the hill country of the Amorites, as the Lord, our God, had commanded; and we came to Kadesh-barnea.
text.DRB: And departing from Horeb, we passed through the terrible and huge wilderness, which you saw, by the way of the mountain of the Amorrhite, as the Lord our God had commanded us. And when we were come into Cadesbarne,

text.NRSV: I said to you, “You have reached the hill country of the Amorites, which the Lord our God is giving us.
text.Rotherham: Then said I unto you,—Ye are come in as far as the hill-country of the Amorites, which Yahweh our God, is giving unto us.
text.KJV: And I said unto you, Ye are come unto the mountain of the Amorites, which the Lord our God doth give unto us.
text.NABRE: I said to you, “You have come to the hill country of the Amorites, which the Lord, our God, is giving us.
text.DRB: Isaid to you: You are come to the mountain of the Amorrhite, which the Lord our God will give to us.

text.NRSV: See, the Lord your God has given the land to you; go up, take possession, as the Lord, the God of your ancestors, has promised you; do not fear or be dismayed.”
text.Rotherham: See thou—Yahweh thy God hath set before thee the land,—go up—take possession as Yahweh the God of thy fathers hath spoken unto thee, do not fear, neither be dismayed.
text.KJV: Behold, the Lord thy God hath set the land before thee: go up and possess it, as the Lord God of thy fathers hath said unto thee; fear not, neither be discouraged.
text.NABRE: See, the Lord, your God, has given this land over to you. Go up and take possession of it, as the Lord, the God of your ancestors, has promised you. Do not fear or be dismayed.”
text.DRB: See the Land which the Lord thy God giveth thee: go up and possess it, as the Lord our God hath spoken to thy fathers: fear not, neither dread you any thing.

text.NRSV: All of you came to me and said, “Let us send men ahead of us to explore the land for us and bring back a report to us regarding the route by which we should go up and the cities we will come to.”
text.Rotherham: And ye came near unto me all of you, and said Let us send men before us, that they may search out for us the land,—and bring us back word, the way by which we must go up, and the cities whereinto we must enter,
text.KJV: And ye came near unto me every one of you, and said, We will send men before us, and they shall search us out the land, and bring us word again by what way we must go up, and into what cities we shall come.
text.NABRE: Then all of you approached me and said, “Let us send men ahead to spy out the land for us and report to us on the road we should follow and the cities we will come upon.”
text.DRB: And you came all unto me, and said: Let us send men that may view the Land, and may bring us word what way we shall ascend, and to what cities to go.

text.NRSV: The plan seemed good to me, and I selected twelve of you, one from each tribe.
text.Rotherham: And the thing, was good in mine eyes,—so I took from among you twelve men, one man for each tribe.
text.KJV: And the saying pleased me well: and I took twelve men of you, one of a tribe:
text.NABRE: Agreeing with the proposal, I took twelve men from your number, one from each tribe.
text.DRB: And because the saying pleased me, I sent of you twelve men, one of every tribe.

text.NRSV: They set out and went up into the hill country, and when they reached the Valley of Eshcol they spied it out
text.Rotherham: And they turned and went up the hill-country, and entered in as far as the ravine of Eshcol, and explored it.
text.KJV: And they turned and went up into the mountain, and came unto the valley of Eshcol, and searched it out.
text.NABRE: They set out into the hill country as far as the Wadi Eshcol, and explored it.
text.DRB: Who when they had gone, and were ascended into the mountains, they came as far as the Valley of cluster: and the Land being viewed,

text.NRSV: and gathered some of the land’s produce, which they brought down to us. They brought back a report to us, and said, “It is a good land that the Lord our God is giving us.”
text.Rotherham: And they took in their hand of the fruit of the country, and bare it down unto us, and brought us back word, and said, Good is the land, which Yahweh our God, is giving unto us.
text.KJV: And they took of the fruit of the land in their hands, and brought it down unto us, and brought us word again, and said, It is a good land which the Lord our God doth give us.
text.NABRE: Then, taking along some of the fruit of the land, they brought it down to us and reported, “The land the Lord, our God, is giving us is good.”
text.DRB: taking of the fruits thereof, to shew the fruitfulness, they brought unto us, and said: The Land is good, which the Lord our God will give us.

text.NRSV: But you were unwilling to go up. You rebelled against the command of the Lord your God;
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless ye were not willing to go up,—but rebelled against the bidding of Yahweh, your God;
text.KJV: Notwithstanding ye would not go up, but rebelled against the commandment of the Lord your God:
text.NABRE: Threats of Revolt. But you refused to go up; you defied the command of the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: And you would not go up, but being incredulous at the word of the Lord our God,

text.NRSV: you grumbled in your tents and said, “It is because the Lord hates us that he has brought us out of the land of Egypt, to hand us over to the Amorites to destroy us.
text.Rotherham: and murmured in your tents and said, Because Yahweh hated us, hath he brought us forth out of the land of Egypt,—to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy us.
text.KJV: And ye murmured in your tents, and said, Because the Lord hated us, he hath brought us forth out of the land of Egypt, to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy us.
text.NABRE: You set to murmuring in your tents, “Out of hatred for us the Lord has brought us out of the land of Egypt, to deliver us into the power of the Amorites and destroy us.
text.DRB: you murmured in your tabernacles, and said: our Lord hateth us, and therefore hath brought us out of the Land of Ægypt, that he might deliver us into the hand of the Amorrhite, and destroy us.

text.NRSV: Where are we headed? Our kindred have made our hearts melt by reporting, ‘The people are stronger and taller than we; the cities are large and fortified up to heaven! We actually saw there the offspring of the Anakim!’”
text.Rotherham: Whither, are, we, to go up? Our own brethren, have made our heart melt saying—A people greater and taller than we, cities large and walled-up into the heavens,—yea, moreover sons of Anakim, have we seen there.
text.KJV: Whither shall we go up? our brethren have discouraged our heart, saying, The people is greater and taller than we; the cities are great and walled up to heaven; and moreover we have seen the sons of the Anakims there.
text.NABRE: What shall we meet with up there? Our men have made our hearts melt by saying, ‘The people are bigger and taller than we, and their cities are large and fortified to the sky; besides, we saw the Anakim there.’”
text.DRB: Whither shall we go up? The messengers have feared our heart, saying: The multitude is very great, and taller of stature than we: the cities great, and fenced even unto heaven, the sons of the Enacims we have seen there.

text.NRSV: I said to you, “Have no dread or fear of them.
text.Rotherham: Then I said unto you,—Ye must not tremble, nor be afraid of them:
text.KJV: Then I said unto you, Dread not, neither be afraid of them.
text.NABRE: But I said to you, “Have no dread or fear of them.
text.DRB: And I said to you: Fear not, neither be ye afraid of them:

text.NRSV: The Lord your God, who goes before you, is the one who will fight for you, just as he did for you in Egypt before your very eyes,
text.Rotherham: Yahweh your God, who is going before you, he, will fight for you,—according to all that he did with you in Egypt, before your eyes;
text.KJV: The Lord your God which goeth before you, he shall fight for you, according to all that he did for you in Egypt before your eyes;
text.NABRE: The Lord, your God, who goes before you, is the one who will fight for you, just as he acted with you before your very eyes in Egypt,
text.DRB: Our Lord God, which is your conductor, himself will fight for you, as he did in Ægypt in the sight of all.

text.NRSV: and in the wilderness, where you saw how the Lord your God carried you, just as one carries a child, all the way that you traveled until you reached this place.
text.Rotherham: also in the desert which thou hast seen, where Yahweh thy God carried thee as a man will carry his son, throughout all the way that ye journeyed, until ye entered as far as, this place.
text.KJV: And in the wilderness, where thou hast seen how that the Lord thy God bare thee, as a man doth bear his son, in all the way that ye went, until ye came into this place.
text.NABRE: as well as in the wilderness, where you saw how the Lord, your God, carried you, as one carries his own child, all along your journey until you arrived at this place.”
text.DRB: And in the wilderness (thyself hast seen) the Lord thy God hath carried thee, as a man is wont to bear his little son, all the way that you have walked, until you came to this place.

text.NRSV: But in spite of this, you have no trust in the Lord your God,
text.Rotherham: But in this thing, ye were putting no trust in Yahweh your God;
text.KJV: Yet in this thing ye did not believe the Lord your God,
text.NABRE: Despite this, you would not trust the Lord, your God,
text.DRB: And neither so did you believe the Lord your God,

text.NRSV: who goes before you on the way to seek out a place for you to camp, in fire by night, and in the cloud by day, to show you the route you should take.”
text.Rotherham: who was going before you in the way—to search out for you a place, where ye might encamp,—in the fire by night, that ye might get sight of the way wherein ye should go, and in the cloud, by day.
text.KJV: Who went in the way before you, to search you out a place to pitch your tents in, in fire by night, to shew you by what way ye should go, and in a cloud by day.
text.NABRE: who journeys before you to find you a place to camp—by night in the fire, and by day in the cloud, to show you the way to go.
text.DRB: who went before you in the way, and marked out the place wherein you should pitch your tents, in the night shewing you the way by fire, and in the day by the pillar of a cloud.

text.NRSV: When the Lord heard your words, he was wrathful and swore:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh heard the voice of your words,—and was wroth and sware—saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord heard the voice of your words, and was wroth, and sware, saying,
text.NABRE: When the Lord heard your words, he was angry, and took an oath:
text.DRB: And when our Lord had heard the voice of your words, being wrath he sware and said:

text.NRSV: “Not one of these—not one of this evil generation—shall see the good land that I swore to give to your ancestors,
text.Rotherham: Surely, not one of these men, this wicked generation,—shall see the good land, which I sware to give unto your fathers:
text.KJV: Surely there shall not one of these men of this evil generation see that good land, which I sware to give unto your fathers,
text.NABRE: Not a single one of this evil generation shall look upon the good land I swore to give to your ancestors,
text.DRB: There shall not any of the men of this wicked generation see the good Land, which by oath I promised to your fathers:

text.NRSV: except Caleb son of Jephunneh. He shall see it, and to him and to his descendants I will give the land on which he set foot, because of his complete fidelity to the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: saving Caleb son of Jephunneh, he, shall see it, and unto him, will I give the land through which he hath trodden, and unto his sons,—because he hath wholly followed Yahweh.
text.KJV: Save Caleb the son of Jephunneh; he shall see it, and to him will I give the land that he hath trodden upon, and to his children, because he hath wholly followed the Lord.
text.NABRE: except Caleb, son of Jephunneh. He shall see it, for to him and to his descendants I will give the land he trod upon, because he has fully followed the Lord.
text.DRB: beside Caleb the son of Jephone, For he shall see it, and to him I will give the Land, that he hath trodden, and to his children, because he hath followed the Lord.

text.NRSV: Even with me the Lord was angry on your account, saying, “You also shall not enter there.
text.Rotherham: Even with me, did Yahweh show himself angry, for your sakes, saying,—Even thou, shalt not enter therein.
text.KJV: Also the Lord was angry with me for your sakes, saying, Thou also shalt not go in thither.
text.NABRE: The Lord was angered against me also on your account, and said, You shall not enter there either,
text.DRB: Neither is his indignation against the people to be marveled at, whereas our Lord being angry with me also for you, said: Neither shalt thou enter in thither.

text.NRSV: Joshua son of Nun, your assistant, shall enter there; encourage him, for he is the one who will secure Israel’s possession of it.
text.Rotherham: Joshua son of Nun, who standeth before thee, he, shall enter in thither, him, strengthen thou, for he, shall cause Israel to inherit it.
text.KJV: But Joshua the son of Nun, which standeth before thee, he shall go in thither: encourage him: for he shall cause Israel to inherit it.
text.NABRE: but Joshua, son of Nun, your attendant, shall enter. Encourage him, for he is the one who is to give Israel its possession.
text.DRB: But Josue the son of Nun thy minister, he shall enter for thee: exhort and strengthen him, and he shall by lot divide the Land of Israel.

text.NRSV: And as for your little ones, who you thought would become booty, your children, who today do not yet know right from wrong, they shall enter there; to them I will give it, and they shall take possession of it.
text.Rotherham: And your little ones, of whom ye said they should become, a prey, and your sons who to-day know not good and evil, they, shall enter in thither,—and unto them, will I give it, and they, shall possess it.
text.KJV: Moreover your little ones, which ye said should be a prey, and your children, which in that day had no knowledge between good and evil, they shall go in thither, and unto them will I give it, and they shall possess it.
text.NABRE: Your little ones, who you said would become plunder, and your children, who as yet do not know good from evil—they shall enter there; to them I will give it, and they shall take possession of it.
text.DRB: Your little ones, of whom you said that they should be led captives, and your sons that this day know not the difference of good and evil, they shall enter in: and to them I will give the Land, and they shall possess it.

text.NRSV: But as for you, journey back into the wilderness, in the direction of the Red Sea.”
text.Rotherham: But as for you, turn ye, for your part,—and set forward towards the desert, by way of the Red Sea.
text.KJV: But as for you, turn you, and take your journey into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea.
text.NABRE: But as for yourselves: turn back and proceed into the wilderness on the Red Sea road.
text.DRB: But return you and go into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea.

text.NRSV: You answered me, “We have sinned against the Lord! We are ready to go up and fight, just as the Lord our God commanded us.” So all of you strapped on your battle gear, and thought it easy to go up into the hill country.
text.Rotherham: Then responded ye and said unto me—We have sinned against Yahweh, We, ourselves, will go up, and fight, according to all that Yahweh our God hath commanded us. And when ye had girded on every man his weapons of war, ye foolishly went up towards the hill-country.
text.KJV: Then ye answered and said unto me, We have sinned against the Lord, we will go up and fight, according to all that the Lord our God commanded us. And when ye had girded on every man his weapons of war, ye were ready to go up into the hill.
text.NABRE: Unsuccessful Invasion. In reply you said to me, “We have sinned against the Lord. We will go up ourselves and fight, just as the Lord, our God, commanded us.” And each of you girded on his weapons, making light of going up into the hill country.
text.DRB: And you answered me: We have sinned to our Lord: We will go up and fight, as the Lord our God hath commanded. And when you ready armed went unto the mountain,

text.NRSV: The Lord said to me, “Say to them, ‘Do not go up and do not fight, for I am not in the midst of you; otherwise you will be defeated by your enemies.’”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me—Say unto them Ye shall not go up, nor fight, for I am not in your midst,—lest ye should be smitten before your enemies.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto me, Say unto them, Go not up, neither fight; for I am not among you; lest ye be smitten before your enemies.
text.NABRE: But the Lord said to me, Warn them: Do not go up and fight—for I will not be in your midst—lest you be beaten down before your enemies.
text.DRB: our Lord said to me: Say to them: Go not up, and fight not, for I am not with you: lest you fall before your enemies.

text.NRSV: Although I told you, you would not listen. You rebelled against the command of the Lord and presumptuously went up into the hill country.
text.Rotherham: So I spake unto you howbeit ye hearkened not,—but rebelled against the bidding of Yahweh, and presumptuously went up the hill-country.
text.KJV: So I spake unto you; and ye would not hear, but rebelled against the commandment of the Lord, and went presumptuously up into the hill.
text.NABRE: I gave you this warning but you would not listen. You defied the Lord ’s command and arrogantly went off into the hill country.
text.DRB: Ispake, and you heard not: But resisting the commandment of our Lord, and swelling with pride you went up into the mountain.

text.NRSV: The Amorites who lived in that hill country then came out against you and chased you as bees do. They beat you down in Seir as far as Hormah.
text.Rotherham: Then came forth the Amorites who were dwelling in that country, to meet you, and they chased you, as do, bees; and struck you down in Seir, as far as Hormah.
text.KJV: And the Amorites, which dwelt in that mountain, came out against you, and chased you, as bees do, and destroyed you in Seir, even unto Hormah.
text.NABRE: Then the Amorites living in that hill country came out against you and put you to flight the way bees do, cutting you down in Seir as far as Hormah.
text.DRB: Therefore the Amorrhite that dwelt in the mountains issuing forth, and coming to meet you, pursued you, as bees are wont to pursue: and smote you from Seir as far as Horma.

text.NRSV: When you returned and wept before the Lord, the Lord would neither heed your voice nor pay you any attention.
text.Rotherham: So then ye returned and wept before Yahweh,—but Yahweh hearkened not unto your voice, nor gave ear unto you.
text.KJV: And ye returned and wept before the Lord; but the Lord would not hearken to your voice, nor give ear unto you.
text.NABRE: On your return you wept before the Lord, but the Lord did not listen to your voice or give ear to you.
text.DRB: And when returning you wept before our Lord, he heard you not, neither would he condescend to your voice.

text.NRSV: After you had stayed at Kadesh as many days as you did,
text.Rotherham: And ye abode in Kadesh many days,—how many were the days that ye abode [there]!
text.KJV: So ye abode in Kadesh many days, according unto the days that ye abode there.
text.NABRE: That is why you had to stay as long as you did at Kadesh.
text.DRB: You abode therefore in Cadesbarne a great time.

text.NRSV: Then we turned back and journeyed into the wilderness, in the direction of the Red Sea, as the Lord had told me, and skirted Mount Seir for many days.
text.Rotherham: Then turned we, and set forward towards the desert by way of the Red Sea, as Yahweh, spake unto me; and we encompassed Mount Seir many days.
text.KJV: Then we turned, and took our journey into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea, as the Lord spake unto me: and we compassed mount Seir many days.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 Northward Along Edom. Then we turned and proceeded into the wilderness on the Red Sea road, as the Lord had told me, and circled around the highlands of Seir for a long time.
text.DRB: And departing thence we came into the wilderness, that leadeth to the Red sea, as our Lord had said to me: and we compassed the mountain Seir a long time.

text.NRSV: And the Lord spoke to me, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto me, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: Finally the Lord said to me,
text.DRB: And our Lord said to me:

text.NRSV: You have been skirting this hill country long enough; turn north,
text.Rotherham: Long enough, have ye encompassed this mountain,—turn yourselves north, wards.
text.KJV: Ye have compassed this mountain long enough: turn you northward.
text.NABRE: You have wandered round these highlands long enough; turn and go north.
text.DRB: It is sufficient for you to have compassed this mountain: go toward the North.

text.NRSV: and command the people: You are about to cross the territory of your kindred, the descendants of Esau, who live in Seir. Though they will be afraid of you, be very careful
text.Rotherham: And, the people, command thou saying, Ye are about to pass through the boundary of your brethren the sons of Esau, who are dwelling in Seir,—and should they be afraid of you, take ye good heed to yourselves:
text.KJV: And command thou the people, saying, Ye are to pass through the coast of your brethren the children of Esau, which dwell in Seir; and they shall be afraid of you: take ye good heed unto yourselves therefore:
text.NABRE: Command the people: You are now about to pass through the territory of your relatives, the descendants of Esau, who live in Seir. Though they are afraid of you, be very careful
text.DRB: And command thou the people, saying: You shall pass by the borders of your brethren the children of Esau, which dwell in Seir, and they will be afraid of you.

text.NRSV: not to engage in battle with them; for I will not give you any of their land, no, not so much as a foot’s length; since I have given Mount Seir to Esau as a possession.
text.Rotherham: do not engage in strife with them, for I will not give you of their land, so much as place for the tread of a foot-sole,—for as a possession to Esau, have I given Mount Seir.
text.KJV: Meddle not with them; for I will not give you of their land, no, not so much as a foot breadth; because I have given mount Seir unto Esau for a possession.
text.NABRE: not to come in conflict with them, for I will not give you so much as a foot of their land, since I have already given Esau possession of the highlands of Seir.
text.DRB: Look diligently therefore that you stir not against them. For I will not give you of their land so much as the step of one foot can tread, because I have given the mountain Seir to be the possession of Esau.

text.NRSV: You shall purchase food from them for money, so that you may eat; and you shall also buy water from them for money, so that you may drink.
text.Rotherham: Food, shall ye buy of them with silver so shall ye eat,—yea even water, shall ye buy of them with silver so shall ye drink.
text.KJV: Ye shall buy meat of them for money, that ye may eat; and ye shall also buy water of them for money, that ye may drink.
text.NABRE: You shall purchase from them with money the food you eat; even the water you drink you shall buy from them with money.
text.DRB: Meats you shall buy of them with money, and shall eat: bought water shall you draw, and drink.

text.NRSV: For the Lord your God has blessed you in all your undertakings; he knows your going through this great wilderness. These forty years the Lord your God has been with you; you have lacked nothing.’
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh thy God, hath blessed thee in all the work of thy hand, he hath considered thy traversing of this great desert,—these forty years, hath Yahweh thy God been with thee, thou hast lacked, nothing.
text.KJV: For the Lord thy God hath blessed thee in all the works of thy hand: he knoweth thy walking through this great wilderness: these forty years the Lord thy God hath been with thee; thou hast lacked nothing.
text.NABRE: Surely, the Lord, your God, has blessed you in all your undertakings; he has been concerned about your journey through this vast wilderness. It is now forty years that the Lord, your God, has been with you, and you have lacked nothing.
text.DRB: The Lord thy God hath blessed thee in every work of thy hands: he knoweth thy journey; how thou hast passed this great wilderness, for forty years the Lord thy God dwelling with thee, and thou hast wanted nothing.

text.NRSV: So we went past our kindred, the descendants of Esau who live in Seir, past the Arabah, past Ezion-geber. And we turned and went in the direction of the wilderness of Moab.
text.Rotherham: So we passed on, a way from our brethren the sons of Esau who dwell in Seir, from the way of the waste plain, from Elath, and from Ezion-geber,—and we turned and passed on by way of the desert of Moab.
text.KJV: And when we passed by from our brethren the children of Esau, which dwelt in Seir, through the way of the plain from Elath, and from Ezion–gaber, we turned and passed by the way of the wilderness of Moab.
text.NABRE: So we passed by our relatives, the descendants of Esau who live in Seir, leaving behind us the Arabah route, Elath, and Ezion-geber. Along Moab. Then we turned and passed on toward the wilderness of Moab.
text.DRB: And when we had passed by our brethren the children of Esau, that dwelt in Seir, by the champion way from Elath and from Asiongaber, we came to the way, that leadeth into the desert of Moab.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to me, ‘Do not harass Moab or engage them in battle, for I will not give you any of their land as a possession, since I have given Ar to the descendants of Lot for a possession.’
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto me—Do not lay siege to Moab, nor engage in strife with them in battle,—for I will not give unto thee of this land as a possession, for unto the sons of Lot, have I given Ar, as a possession.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto me, Distress not the Moabites, neither contend with them in battle: for I will not give thee of their land for a possession; because I have given Ar unto the children of Lot for a possession.
text.NABRE: And the Lord said to me, Do not show hostility to the Moabites or engage them in battle, for I will not give you possession of any of their land, since I have given Ar to the descendants of Lot as their possession.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to me: Fight not against the Moabites, neither make battle against them: for I will not give thee any of their land, because I have given Ar to the children of Lot in possession.

text.NRSV: (The Emim, a large and numerous people, as tall as the Anakim, had formerly inhabited it.
text.Rotherham: the Emim, formerly dwelt therein,—a people great and many and tall, like the Anakim.
text.KJV: The Emims dwelt therein in times past, a people great, and many, and tall, as the Anakims;
text.NABRE: (Formerly the Emim lived there, a people great and numerous and as tall as the Anakim;
text.DRB: Emim first were the inhabiters thereof, a great people, and valiant, and so tall that they were thought,

text.NRSV: Like the Anakim, they were reckoned as Rephaim, but the Moabites called them Emim.
text.Rotherham: Giants, used, they also, to be accounted like the Anakim,—but the Moabites, called them Emim.
text.KJV: Which also were accounted giants, as the Anakims; but the Moabites call them Emims.
text.NABRE: like the Anakim they are considered Rephaim, though the Moabites call them Emim.
text.DRB: as it were giants, of the Enacims' stock, and were like the children of the Enacims. Moreover the Moabites call them Emim.

text.NRSV: Formerly the Horites lived in Seir, but the descendants of Esau dispossessed them and destroyed them and settled in their place, as Israel did in the land that the Lord gave them as their possession.)
text.Rotherham: And in Seir, dwelt the Horim aforetime, but, the sons of Esau, dispossessed them, and destroyed them from before them, and dwelt in their stead,—as did Israel unto the land of his own possession, which Yahweh had given unto them.
text.KJV: The Horims also dwelt in Seir beforetime; but the children of Esau succeeded them, when they had destroyed them from before them, and dwelt in their stead; as Israel did unto the land of his possession, which the Lord gave unto them.
text.NABRE: In Seir, however, the former inhabitants were the Horites; the descendants of Esau dispossessed them, clearing them out of the way and dwelling in their place, just as Israel has done in the land of its possession which the Lord gave it.)
text.DRB: But in Seir before dwelt the Horrins: who being expelled and destroyed, the children of Esau did inhabit it, as Israel did in the land of his possession, which our Lord gave him.

text.NRSV: ‘Now rise up, set out on your journey, and cross the Wadi Zered.’ So we crossed the Wadi Zered.
text.Rotherham: Now, arise, and pass ye over the ravine of Zered. So we passed over the ravine of Zered.
text.KJV: Now rise up, said I, and get you over the brook Zered. And we went over the brook Zered.
text.NABRE: Now get ready to cross the Wadi Zered. So we crossed the Wadi Zered.
text.DRB: Rising up therefore to pass the Torrent Zared, we came to it.

text.NRSV: The time from our leaving Kadesh-barnea until we crossed the Wadi Zered was thirty-eight years, until the whole generation that had done evil in the sight of the Lord had perished from the camp, as the Lord had sworn to them.
text.Rotherham: Now, the days in which we journeyed from Kadesh-barnea, as far as where we crossed the ravine of Zered, were thirty-eight years,—until all the generation of the men of war were consumed out of the midst of the camp, as Yahweh had sworn unto them.
text.KJV: And the space in which we came from Kadesh–barnea, until we were come over the brook Zered, was thirty and eight years; until all the generation of the men of war were wasted out from among the host, as the Lord sware unto them.
text.NABRE: Now thirty-eight years had elapsed between our departure from Kadesh-barnea and the crossing of the Wadi Zered; in the meantime the whole generation of soldiers had perished from the camp, as the Lord had sworn they should.
text.DRB: And the time, that we walked from Cadesbarne unto the passage of the torrent Zared, was thirty and eight years: until all the generation of the men that were warriors was consumed out of the camp, as our Lord had sworn:

text.NRSV: Indeed, the hand of the Lord was against them, to root them out from the camp, until all had perished.
text.Rotherham: Yea, even the hand of Yahweh was against them, to destroy them out of the midst of the camp,—until they were consumed.
text.KJV: For indeed the hand of the Lord was against them, to destroy them from among the host, until they were consumed.
text.NABRE: Indeed the Lord ’s own hand was against them, to rout them from the camp completely.
text.DRB: whose hand was against them, that they should perish from among the camp.

text.NRSV: And as soon as all the warriors had perished from the people,
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass when all the men of war were consumed by dying, out of the midst of the people,
text.KJV: So it came to pass, when all the men of war were consumed and dead from among the people,
text.NABRE: Along Ammon. When at length death had put an end to all the soldiers among the people,
text.DRB: And after all the warriors were dead,

text.NRSV: the Lord spoke to me, saying,
text.Rotherham: then spake Yahweh unto me, saying:
text.KJV: That the Lord spake unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: the Lord said to me,
text.DRB: our Lord spake to me, saying:

text.NRSV: ‘Today you are going to cross the boundary of Moab at Ar.
text.Rotherham: Thou, art passing, to-day, the boundary of Moab even Ar;
text.KJV: Thou art to pass over through Ar, the coast of Moab, this day:
text.NABRE: You are now about to leave Ar and the territory of Moab behind.
text.DRB: Thou shalt pass this day the borders of Moab, the city named Ar:

text.NRSV: When you approach the frontier of the Ammonites, do not harass them or engage them in battle, for I will not give you any of the land of the Ammonites as a possession, since I have given it to the descendants of Lot for a possession.’
text.Rotherham: so wilt thou draw near over against the sons of Ammon, do not thou besiege them neither engage in strife with them,—for I will not give of the land of the sons of Ammon unto thee as a possession, for unto the sons of Lot, have I given it as a possession.
text.KJV: And when thou comest nigh over against the children of Ammon, distress them not, nor meddle with them: for I will not give thee of the land of the children of Ammon any possession; because I have given it unto the children of Lot for a possession.
text.NABRE: As you come opposite the Ammonites, do not show hostility or come in conflict with them, for I will not give you possession of any land of the Ammonites, since I have given it to the descendants of Lot as their possession.
text.DRB: and approaching unto the frontiers of the children of Ammon, beware thou fight not against them, neither once move to battle: for I will not give thee of the land of the children of Ammon, because I have given it to the children of Lot in possession.

text.NRSV: (That also was reckoned a land of Rephaim. Rephaim formerly lived there—but the Ammonites call them Zamzummim,
text.Rotherham: A land of giants, used, that also, to be accounted,—giants, dwelt therein aforetime, but the Ammonites, called them Zamzummim:
text.KJV: (That also was accounted a land of giants: giants dwelt therein in old time; and the Ammonites call them Zamzummims;
text.NABRE: (This also is considered a country of the Rephaim; formerly the Rephaim dwelt there. The Ammonites call them Zamzummim,
text.DRB: It was reputed the land of giants: and giants in old time dwelt in it, whom the Ammonites call Zomzommim,

text.NRSV: a people as numerous and tall as the Anakim; but the Lord destroyed them so that they could not continue to live there; so they lived in their place instead of them, as they have done to this day.
text.Rotherham: a people great and many and tall, like the Anakim,—but Yahweh destroyed them from before them, and they took their possessions, and dwelt in their stead:
text.KJV: A people great, and many, and tall, as the Anakims; but the Lord destroyed them before them; and they succeeded them, and dwelt in their stead:
text.NABRE: a people great and numerous and as tall as the Anakim. But these, too, the Lord cleared out of the way for the Ammonites, so that they dispossessed them and dwelt in their place.
text.DRB: a great and huge people, & of long stature, as the Enacims whom our Lord destroyed before their face: and he made them to dwell in their stead,

text.NRSV: He did the same for the descendants of Esau who live in Seir, when he destroyed the Horites before them; they dispossessed them and settled in their place until today.
text.Rotherham: as he did for the sons of Esau, who are dwelling in Seir,—when he destroyed the Horim from before them, and they took their possessions and have dwelt in their stead, even unto this day.
text.KJV: As he did to the children of Esau, which dwelt in Seir, when he destroyed the Horims from before them; and they succeeded them, and dwelt in their stead even unto this day:
text.NABRE: He did the same for the descendants of Esau, who live in Seir, by clearing the Horites out of their way, so that they dispossessed them and dwelt in their place down to the present.
text.DRB: as he had done to the children of Esau, that dwelt in Seir, destroying the Horrheites, and delivering their land to them, which they possess until this present.

text.NRSV: As for the Avvim, who lived in villages as far as Gaza, the Caphtorim, who came from Caphtor, destroyed them and settled in their place.)
text.Rotherham: The Avvim also, who dwelt in settlements as far as Gaza, Caphtorim, who were coming forth out of Caphtor, destroyed them, and dwelt in their stead.
text.KJV: And the Avims which dwelt in Hazerim, even unto Azzah, the Caphtorims, which came forth out of Caphtor, destroyed them, and dwelt in their stead.)
text.NABRE: As for the Avvim, who once lived in villages in the vicinity of Gaza, the Caphtorim, migrating from Caphtor, cleared them away and dwelt in their place.)
text.DRB: The Heveites also, that dwelt in Haserim as far as Gaza, the Capadocians expelled: who issuing out of Capadocia, destroyed them, and dwelt in their stead.

text.NRSV: ‘Rise up, set out on your journey, and cross the Wadi Arnon. See, I have given into your hand King Sihon the Amorite of Heshbon, and his land. Begin to take possession of it, and engage him in battle.
text.Rotherham: Arise ye, set forward and cross over the ravine of Arnon, see! I have given into thy hand—Sihon king of Heshbon, the Amorite and his land, begin—take possession,—and engage in strife with him in battle.
text.KJV: Rise ye up, take your journey, and pass over the river Arnon: behold, I have given into thine hand Sihon the Amorite, king of Heshbon, and his land: begin to possess it, and contend with him in battle.
text.NABRE: Defeat of Sihon. Advance now across the Wadi Arnon. I now deliver into your power Sihon, the Amorite king of Heshbon, and his land. Begin to take possession; engage him in battle.
text.DRB: Arise ye, and pass the torrent Arnon: behold I have delivered in thy hand Sehon king of Hesebon the Amorrhite, and begin to possess his land, and make war against him.

text.NRSV: Today I will begin to put the dread and fear of you on the nations everywhere under heaven, who on hearing the news of you will tremble and be in anguish because of you.’
text.Rotherham: This day, will I begin to extend the dread of thee and the fear of thee over the face of the peoples under all the heavens,—who will hear the report of thee, then will they quake and writhe in pain because of thee.
text.KJV: This day will I begin to put the dread of thee and the fear of thee upon the nations that are under the whole heaven, who shall hear report of thee, and shall tremble, and be in anguish because of thee.
text.NABRE: This day I will begin to put a fear and dread of you into the peoples everywhere under heaven, so that at the mention of your name they will quake and tremble before you.
text.DRB: This day will I begin to send thy terror and fear upon the peoples, that dwell under the whole heaven: that hearing thy name they may quake, and tremble after the manner of women in travail, and be pinched with sorrow.

text.NRSV: So I sent messengers from the wilderness of Kedemoth to King Sihon of Heshbon with an offer of peace, saying,
text.Rotherham: So I sent messengers out of the desert of Kademoth, unto Sihon, king of Heshbon,—with words of peace saying:
text.KJV: And I sent messengers out of the wilderness of Kedemoth unto Sihon king of Heshbon with words of peace, saying,
text.NABRE: So I sent messengers from the wilderness of Kedemoth to Sihon, king of Heshbon, with this offer of peace:
text.DRB: Isent therefore messengers from the wilderness of Cademoth to Sehon the king of Hesebon with peaceable words, saying:

text.NRSV: ‘Let me pass through your land by the road. I will go only by the road. I will not turn aside to the right or to the left;
text.Rotherham: I would pass along through thy land, by the road, by the road, will I go,—I will not turn aside, to the right hand or to the left:
text.KJV: Let me pass through thy land: I will go along by the high way, I will neither turn unto the right hand nor to the left.
text.NABRE: “Let me pass through your country. I will travel only on the road. I will not turn aside either to the right or to the left.
text.DRB: We will pass through thy land, we will go the common high way: we will not decline neither to the right hand, nor to the left.

text.NRSV: you shall sell me food for money, so that I may eat, and give me water for money, so that I may drink. Only allow me to pass through on foot,
text.Rotherham: Food for silver, shalt thou sell me so will I eat, and water for silver, shalt thou let me have, so will I drink,—only I would pass through on my feet;—
text.KJV: Thou shalt sell me meat for money, that I may eat; and give me water for money, that I may drink: only I will pass through on my feet;
text.NABRE: The food I eat you will sell me for money, and the water I drink, you will give me for money. Only let me march through,
text.DRB: Sell us meats for money, that we may eat: give us water for money, and so we will drink. Only this, that thou wilt grant us passage,

text.NRSV: just as the descendants of Esau who live in Seir did for me, and the Moabites who live in Ar, until I cross the Jordan into the land that the Lord our God is giving us.’
text.Rotherham: as the sons of Esau, who are dwelling in Seir, did to me, and the Moabites who are dwelling in Ar,—until that I pass over the Jordan, into the land which, Yahweh our God, is giving unto us.
text.KJV: (As the children of Esau which dwell in Seir, and the Moabites which dwell in Ar, did unto me;) until I shall pass over Jordan into the land which the Lord our God giveth us.
text.NABRE: as the descendants of Esau who dwell in Seir and the Moabites who dwell in Ar have done, until I cross the Jordan into the land the Lord, our God, is about to give us.”
text.DRB: as the children of Esau have done, that dwell in Seir, and the Moabites, that abide in Ar: until we come to Jordan, and pass to the Land, which the Lord our God will give us.

text.NRSV: But King Sihon of Heshbon was not willing to let us pass by him, for the Lord your God hardened his spirit and made his heart defiant, in order to give him into your hand, as he has now done.
text.Rotherham: But Sihon king of Heshbon, was not willing, to let us pass along through it,—for Yahweh thy God had suffered him to make his spirit harsh and his heart bold, that he might give him into thy hand—(as [appeareth] this day).
text.KJV: But Sihon king of Heshbon would not let us pass by him: for the Lord thy God hardened his spirit, and made his heart obstinate, that he might deliver him into thy hand, as appeareth this day.
text.NABRE: But Sihon, king of Heshbon, refused to let us pass through his land, because the Lord, your God, made him stubborn in mind and obstinate in heart that he might deliver him into your power, as indeed he has now done.
text.DRB: And Sehon the king of Hesebon would not give us passage: because the Lord thy God had indurated his spirit, and hardened his heart, that he might be delivered into thy hands, as now thou seest.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to me, ‘See, I have begun to give Sihon and his land over to you; begin now to take possession of his land.’
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me, See! I have begun to deliver up before thee Sihon and his land,—begin take possession, that thou mayest make a possession of his land.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto me, Behold, I have begun to give Sihon and his land before thee: begin to possess, that thou mayest inherit his land.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to me, Now that I have already begun to give over to you Sihon and his land, begin to take possession.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to me: Behold I have begun to deliver Sehon unto thee, and his land, begin to possess it.

text.NRSV: Then Sihon came out against us, he and all his people, to battle at Jahaz.
text.Rotherham: So then Sihon came out to meet us he, and all his people, to give battle at Jahaz.
text.KJV: Then Sihon came out against us, he and all his people, to fight at Jahaz.
text.NABRE: So Sihon and all his people advanced against us to join battle at Jahaz;
text.DRB: And Sehon came forth to meet us with all his people to battle in Jasa.

text.NRSV: The Lord our God gave him over to us; and we struck him down, along with his sons and his whole people.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh our God delivered him up before us,—and we smote him and his sons, and all his people
text.KJV: And the Lord our God delivered him before us; and we smote him, and his sons, and all his people.
text.NABRE: but since the Lord, our God, had given him over to us, we defeated him and his sons and all his people.
text.DRB: And the Lord our God delivered him to us: and we smote him with his sons and all his people.

text.NRSV: At that time we captured all his towns, and in each town we utterly destroyed men, women, and children. We left no survivors.
text.Rotherham: And we captured all his cities, at that time, and devoted to destruction every city of males, with the women and the little ones,—we left not remaining a survivor:
text.KJV: And we took all his cities at that time, and utterly destroyed the men, and the women, and the little ones, of every city, we left none to remain:
text.NABRE: At that time we captured all his cities and put every city under the ban, men, women and children; we left no survivor.
text.DRB: And all his cities we took at that time, killing the inhabiters thereof, men and women and little ones. We left nothing among them.

text.NRSV: We took only the livestock as spoil for ourselves, with the plunder of the towns that we captured.
text.Rotherham: only the cattle, made we our prey,—and the spoil of the cities which we captured.
text.KJV: Only the cattle we took for a prey unto ourselves, and the spoil of the cities which we took.
text.NABRE: Our only plunder was the livestock and the spoils of the captured cities.
text.DRB: Except the cattle, which came to their portion that took preys: and the spoils of the cities, which we took

text.NRSV: From Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon (and from the town in the wadi itself), as far as Gilead, there was not a town too high for us; the Lord our God gave us all of them.
text.Rotherham: From Aroer, which is on the edge of the ravine of Arnon and the city that is in the ravine even as far as Gilead, there was not a fortress that proved too high for us,—the whole, did Yahweh our God deliver up before us.
text.KJV: From Aroer, which is by the brink of the river of Arnon, and from the city that is by the river, even unto Gilead, there was not one city too strong for us: the Lord our God delivered all unto us:
text.NABRE: From Aroer on the edge of the Wadi Arnon and from the town in the wadi itself, as far as Gilead, no city was too well fortified for us. All of them the Lord, our God, gave over to us.
text.DRB: from Aroer, which is upon the bank of the torrent Arnon, a town that is situated in a valley, as far as Galaad. There was not a village or city, that escaped our hands: the Lord our God delivered all unto us.

text.NRSV: You did not encroach, however, on the land of the Ammonites, avoiding the whole upper region of the Wadi Jabbok as well as the towns of the hill country, just as the Lord our God had charged.
text.Rotherham: Only unto the land of the sons of Ammon, didst thou not come near,—all the side of the Jabbok ravine, nor the cities of the hill country, nor any which Yahweh our God had forbidden to us.
text.KJV: Only unto the land of the children of Ammon thou camest not, nor unto any place of the river Jabbok, nor unto the cities in the mountains, nor unto whatsoever the Lord our God forbad us.
text.NABRE: However, just as the Lord, our God, commanded us, you did not encroach upon any of the Ammonite land, neither the region bordering on the Wadi Jabbok, nor the cities of the highlands.
text.DRB: Except the land of the children of Ammon, to the which we approached not: and all that adjoin to the torrent Jeboc, and the cities on the mountain, and all the places, from which the Lord our God prohibited us.

text.NRSV: When we headed up the road to Bashan, King Og of Bashan came out against us, he and all his people, for battle at Edrei.
text.Rotherham: Then turned we and went up, the way of Bashan; and Og king of Bashan, came out to meet us, he, and all his people, to give battle at Edrei.
text.KJV: Then we turned, and went up the way to Bashan: and Og the king of Bashan came out against us, he and all his people, to battle at Edrei.
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 Defeat of Og. Then we turned and proceeded up the road to Bashan. But Og, king of Bashan, came out against us with all his people to give battle at Edrei.
text.DRB: Therefore turning we went up by the way of Basan: and Og the king of Basan came forth to meet us with his people to fight in Edrai.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to me, “Do not fear him, for I have handed him over to you, along with his people and his land. Do to him as you did to King Sihon of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto me: Do not fear him, for into thy hand, have I delivered him and all his people and his land,—so then thou shalt do unto him, as thou didst unto Sihon king of the Amorites who was dwelling in Heshbon.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto me, Fear him not: for I will deliver him, and all his people, and his land, into thy hand; and thou shalt do unto him as thou didst unto Sihon king of the Amorites, which dwelt at Heshbon.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to me, Do not be afraid of him, for I have delivered him into your power with all his people and his land. Do to him as you did to Sihon, king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to me: Fear him not: because he is delivered into thy hand with all his people and his land: and thou shalt do to him as thou hast done to Sehon king of the Amorrheites, that dwelt in Hesebon.

text.NRSV: So the Lord our God also handed over to us King Og of Bashan and all his people. We struck him down until not a single survivor was left.
text.Rotherham: Then did Yahweh our God deliver into our hand, Og also, king of Bashan and all his people,—so we smote him until there was not left remaining to him a survivor.
text.KJV: So the Lord our God delivered into our hands Og also, the king of Bashan, and all his people: and we smote him until none was left to him remaining.
text.NABRE: And thus the Lord, our God, delivered into our power also Og, king of Bashan, with all his people. We defeated him so completely that we left him no survivor.
text.DRB: Therefore the Lord our God delivered into our hands Og also the king of Basan, and all his people: and we struck them to utter destruction,

text.NRSV: At that time we captured all his towns; there was no citadel that we did not take from them—sixty towns, the whole region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
text.Rotherham: And we captured all his cities, at that time, there was no fortress which we took not from them,—sixty cities all the region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
text.KJV: And we took all his cities at that time, there was not a city which we took not from them, threescore cities, all the region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
text.NABRE: At that time we captured all his cities; there was no town we did not take: sixty cities in all, the whole region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan—
text.DRB: wasting all his cities at one time. There was not a town that escaped us: sixty cities, all the country of Argob the Kingdom of Og in Basan.

text.NRSV: All these were fortress towns with high walls, double gates, and bars, besides a great many villages.
text.Rotherham: All these, were fortified cities each with a high wall, folding gates and a bar,—besides country towns exceeding many.
text.KJV: All these cities were fenced with high walls, gates, and bars; beside unwalled towns a great many.
text.NABRE: all these cities were fortified with high walls and gates and bars—besides a great number of unwalled towns.
text.DRB: All the cities were fenced with very high walls, and with gates and bars, beside innumerable towns that had no walls.

text.NRSV: And we utterly destroyed them, as we had done to King Sihon of Heshbon, in each city utterly destroying men, women, and children.
text.Rotherham: And we devoted them to destruction, doing unto them as we did unto Sihon king of Heshbon,—devoting to destruction every city of males, the women, and the little ones.
text.KJV: And we utterly destroyed them, as we did unto Sihon king of Heshbon, utterly destroying the men, women, and children, of every city.
text.NABRE: As we had done to Sihon, king of Heshbon, so also here we put all the towns under the ban, men, women and children;
text.DRB: And we destroyed them, as we had done to Sehon the king of Hesebon, destroying every city, and men and women and children:

text.NRSV: But all the livestock and the plunder of the towns we kept as spoil for ourselves.
text.Rotherham: But all the cattle, and the spoil of the cities, made we our prey.
text.KJV: But all the cattle, and the spoil of the cities, we took for a prey to ourselves.
text.NABRE: but all the livestock and the spoils of each city we took as plunder for ourselves.
text.DRB: but the cattle, and the spoils of the cities we took for our prey.

text.NRSV: So at that time we took from the two kings of the Amorites the land beyond the Jordan, from the Wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon
text.Rotherham: Thus did we at that time take the land out of the hand of the two kings of the Amorites (which was over the Jordan),—from the ravine of Arnon as far as Mount Hermon.
text.KJV: And we took at that time out of the hand of the two kings of the Amorites the land that was on this side Jordan, from the river of Arnon unto mount Hermon;
text.NABRE: And so at that time we took from the two kings of the Amorites beyond the Jordan the territory from the Wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon
text.DRB: And we took at that time the land out of the hand of two kings of the Amorrheites, that were beyond Jordan: from the torrent Arnon unto the mountain Hermon.

text.NRSV: (the Sidonians call Hermon Sirion, while the Amorites call it Senir),
text.Rotherham: Sidonians, call Hermon Sirion,—but, the Amorites, call it Senir.
text.KJV: (Which Hermon the Sidonians call Sirion; and the Amorites call it Shenir;)
text.NABRE: (the Sidonians call Hermon Sirion and the Amorites call it Senir),
text.DRB: which the Sidonians call Sarion, and the Amorrheites Sanir:

text.NRSV: all the towns of the tableland, the whole of Gilead, and all of Bashan, as far as Salecah and Edrei, towns of Og’s kingdom in Bashan.
text.Rotherham: All the cities of the table-land, and all Gilead, and all Bashan,—unto Salecah, and Edrei,—cities of the kingdom of Og, in Bashan.
text.KJV: All the cities of the plain, and all Gilead, and all Bashan, unto Salchah and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
text.NABRE: all the towns of the plateau, all of Gilead, and all of Bashan as far as Salecah and Edrei, towns of the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
text.DRB: all the cities, that are situated in the plain, and all the Land of Galaad and Basan as far as Selcha, and Edrai, cities of the Kingdom of Og in Basan.

text.NRSV: (Now only King Og of Bashan was left of the remnant of the Rephaim. In fact his bed, an iron bed, can still be seen in Rabbah of the Ammonites. By the common cubit it is nine cubits long and four cubits wide.)
text.Rotherham: For, only Og, king of Bashan was left remaining of the remnant of the giants, lo! his bedstead, was a bedstead of iron, is not, the same, in Rabbath of the sons of Ammon? nine cubits, the length thereof and four cubits, the breadth thereof, by the fore-arm of a man.
text.KJV: For only Og king of Bashan remained of the remnant of giants; behold, his bedstead was a bedstead of iron; is it not in Rabbath of the children of Ammon? nine cubits was the length thereof, and four cubits the breadth of it, after the cubit of a man.
text.NABRE: (Og, king of Bashan, was the last remaining survivor of the Rephaim. He had a bed of iron, nine regular cubits long and four wide, which is still preserved in Rabbah of the Ammonites.)
text.DRB: For only Og the king of Basan remained of the stock of giants. His bed of iron is shewed, which is in Rabbath of the children of Ammon, having nine cubits in length, and four in breadth after the measure of the cubit of a man's hand.

text.NRSV: As for the land that we took possession of at that time, I gave to the Reubenites and Gadites the territory north of Aroer, that is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, as well as half the hill country of Gilead with its towns,
text.Rotherham: And this land, took we in possession at that time,—from Aroer which is by the ravine of Arnon, and half the hill-country of Gilead and the cities thereof, gave I unto the Reubenites and unto the Gadites;
text.KJV: And this land, which we possessed at that time, from Aroer, which is by the river Arnon, and half mount Gilead, and the cities thereof, gave I unto the Reubenites and to the Gadites.
text.NABRE: Allotment of the Conquered Lands. As for the land we took possession of at that time, I gave Reuben and Gad the territory from Aroer, on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, halfway up into the highlands of Gilead, with its cities.
text.DRB: And we possessed the Land at that time from Aroer, which is upon the bank of the torrent Arnon, unto the half part of mount Galaad: and the cities thereof I gave to Ruben and Gad.

text.NRSV: and I gave to the half-tribe of Manasseh the rest of Gilead and all of Bashan, Og’s kingdom. (The whole region of Argob: all that portion of Bashan used to be called a land of Rephaim;
text.Rotherham: but the remainder of Gilead, and all Bashan the kingdom of Og, gave I unto the half tribe of Manasseh,—all the region of the Argob with all Bashan. The same, is called, A land of giants.
text.KJV: And the rest of Gilead, and all Bashan, being the kingdom of Og, gave I unto the half tribe of Manasseh; all the region of Argob, with all Bashan, which was called the land of giants.
text.NABRE: The rest of Gilead and all of Bashan, the kingdom of Og, I gave to the half-tribe of Manasseh. (The whole Argob region, all that part of Bashan, was once called a land of the Rephaim.
text.DRB: And the other part of Galaad, and all Basan of the kingdom of Og, I delivered to the half tribe of Manasses, all the country of Argob: and all Basan is called the Land of giants.

text.NRSV: Jair the Manassite acquired the whole region of Argob as far as the border of the Geshurites and the Maacathites, and he named them—that is, Bashan—after himself, Havvoth-jair, as it is to this day.)
text.Rotherham: Jair, son of Manasseh, took all the region of Argob, as far as the boundary of the Geshurites, and the Maachathites,—and called them after his own name The Bashan of Havvoth-jair unto this day.
text.KJV: Jair the son of Manasseh took all the country of Argob unto the coasts of Geshuri and Maachathi; and called them after his own name, Bashan–havoth–jair, unto this day.
text.NABRE: Jair, a Manassite, took all the region of Argob as far as the border of the Geshurites and Maacathites, and named them—Bashan, that is—after himself, Havvoth-jair, the name it bears today.)
text.DRB: Jair the son of Manasses possessed all the country of Argob unto the borders of Gessuri, and Machati. And he called Basan by his own name, Havoth Jair, that is to say, the towns of Jair, until this present day.

text.NRSV: To Machir I gave Gilead.
text.Rotherham: And, unto Machir, gave I Gilead;
text.KJV: And I gave Gilead unto Machir.
text.NABRE: To Machir I gave Gilead,
text.DRB: To Machir also I gave Galaad.

text.NRSV: And to the Reubenites and the Gadites I gave the territory from Gilead as far as the Wadi Arnon, with the middle of the wadi as a boundary, and up to the Jabbok, the wadi being boundary of the Ammonites;
text.Rotherham: And unto the Reubenites and unto the Gadites, gave I—from Gilead even as far as the ravine of Arnon, the middle of the ravine and boundary,—even as far as the Jabbok ravine, the boundary of the sons of Ammon;
text.KJV: And unto the Reubenites and unto the Gadites I gave from Gilead even unto the river Arnon half the valley, and the border even unto the river Jabbok, which is the border of the children of Ammon;
text.NABRE: and to Reuben and Gad the territory from Gilead to the Wadi Arnon—the middle of the wadi being its boundary—and to the Wadi Jabbok, which is the border of the Ammonites,
text.DRB: And to the tribes of Ruben and Gad I gave of the Land of Galaad as far as the Torrent Arnon, half of the torrent, and the confines unto the torrent Jeboc, which is the border of the children of Ammon:

text.NRSV: the Arabah also, with the Jordan and its banks, from Chinnereth down to the sea of the Arabah, the Dead Sea, with the lower slopes of Pisgah on the east.
text.Rotherham: the Waste Plain also and the Jordan and boundary,—from Chinnereth, even as far as the sea of the Waste Plain the Salt Sea, under the slopes of Pisgah, on the east.
text.KJV: The plain also, and Jordan, and the coast thereof, from Chinnereth even unto the sea of the plain, even the salt sea, under Ashdoth–pisgah eastward.
text.NABRE: as well as the Arabah with the Jordan and its banks from Chinnereth to the Salt Sea of the Arabah, under the slopes of Pisgah on the east.
text.DRB: and the plain of the wilderness, and Jordan, and the borders of Cenereth unto the sea of the desert, which is most salt, at the foot of mount Phasga against the east.

text.NRSV: At that time, I charged you as follows: “Although the Lord your God has given you this land to occupy, all your troops shall cross over armed as the vanguard of your Israelite kin.
text.Rotherham: So I commanded you, at that time saying,—Yahweh your God hath given unto you this land to possess it, armed, shall ye pass over before your brethren the sons of Israel all the sons of valour,
text.KJV: And I commanded you at that time, saying, The Lord your God hath given you this land to possess it: ye shall pass over armed before your brethren the children of Israel, all that are meet for the war.
text.NABRE: At that time I charged you: The Lord, your God, has given you this land as your possession. But all your troops equipped for battle must cross over in the vanguard of your fellow Israelites.
text.DRB: And I commanded you at that time, saying: The Lord your God giveth you this land for an inheritance, go well appointed before your brethren the children of Israel all you strong men:

text.NRSV: Only your wives, your children, and your livestock—I know that you have much livestock—shall stay behind in the towns that I have given to you.
text.Rotherham: Only, your wives and your little ones and your cattle—I know that ye have much cattle—shall abide in your cities which I have given unto you;
text.KJV: But your wives, and your little ones, and your cattle, (for I know that ye have much cattle,) shall abide in your cities which I have given you;
text.NABRE: But your wives and children, as well as your livestock, of which I know you have a large number, shall remain behind in the towns I have given you,
text.DRB: except your wives, and little ones, and your cattle. For I know you have much cattle, & they must remain in the cities, which I have delivered you,

text.NRSV: When the Lord gives rest to your kindred, as to you, and they too have occupied the land that the Lord your God is giving them beyond the Jordan, then each of you may return to the property that I have given to you.”
text.Rotherham: until that Yahweh shall give rest unto your brethren, as well as you, so shall, they too, possess the land which, Yahweh your God, is giving unto them, over the Jordan,—then shall ye return every man unto his possession, which I have given unto you.
text.KJV: Until the Lord have given rest unto your brethren, as well as unto you, and until they also possess the land which the Lord your God hath given them beyond Jordan: and then shall ye return every man unto his possession, which I have given you.
text.NABRE: until the Lord has settled your relatives as well, and they too possess the land which the Lord, your God, will give them on the other side of the Jordan. Then you may all return to the possessions I have given you.
text.DRB: until our Lord give rest to your brethren, as he hath given to you: and they also possess the Land, which he will give them beyond Jordan: then shall every man return to his possession, which I have given you.

text.NRSV: And I charged Joshua as well at that time, saying: “Your own eyes have seen everything that the Lord your God has done to these two kings; so the Lord will do to all the kingdoms into which you are about to cross.
text.Rotherham: Joshua also, commanded I, at that time saying,—Thine are the eyes, that have seen all that Yahweh your God hath done unto these two kings, thus, will Yahweh do unto all the kingdoms whereunto thou, art passing over:
text.KJV: And I commanded Joshua at that time, saying, Thine eyes have seen all that the Lord your God hath done unto these two kings: so shall the Lord do unto all the kingdoms whither thou passest.
text.NABRE: And I charged Joshua as well, “Your own eyes have seen all that the Lord, your God, has done to both these kings; so, too, will the Lord do to all the kingdoms into which you will cross over.
