text.DRB: This day our Lord thy God hath commanded thee to do these commandments and judgments: that thou keep and fulfill them with all thy heart, and with all thy soul.

text.NRSV: Today you have obtained the Lord’s agreement: to be your God; and for you to walk in his ways, to keep his statutes, his commandments, and his ordinances, and to obey him.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast avowed to-day that Yahweh, shall be, thy God, and that thou wilt walk in his ways, and keep his statutes and his commandments and his regulations and wilt hearken unto his voice.
text.KJV: Thou hast avouched the Lord this day to be thy God, and to walk in his ways, and to keep his statutes, and his commandments, and his judgments, and to hearken unto his voice:
text.NABRE: Today you have accepted the Lord ’s agreement: he will be your God, and you will walk in his ways, observe his statutes, commandments, and ordinances, and obey his voice.
text.DRB: Thou hast chosen our Lord this day to be thy God, and to walk in his ways, and keep his ceremonies, and precepts, and judgements, & obey his commandment.

text.NRSV: Today the Lord has obtained your agreement: to be his treasured people, as he promised you, and to keep his commandments;
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, hath avowed to-day that thou shalt be, his people, as a treasure, as he spake unto thee,—and that thou shalt observe all his commandments;
text.KJV: And the Lord hath avouched thee this day to be his peculiar people, as he hath promised thee, and that thou shouldest keep all his commandments;
text.NABRE: And today the Lord has accepted your agreement: you will be a people specially his own, as he promised you, you will keep all his commandments,
text.DRB: And our Lord hath chosen thee this day, that thou shouldst be his peculiar people, as he hath spoken to thee, and thou shouldst keep all his commandments:

text.NRSV: for him to set you high above all nations that he has made, in praise and in fame and in honor; and for you to be a people holy to the Lord your God, as he promised.
text.Rotherham: and [hath avowed] that he will set thee on high above all the nations which he hath made, for praise, and for renown and for glory, and that thou shalt be a holy people unto Yahweh thy God as he hath spoken.
text.KJV: And to make thee high above all nations which he hath made, in praise, and in name, and in honour; and that thou mayest be an holy people unto the Lord thy God, as he hath spoken.
text.NABRE: and he will set you high in praise and renown and glory above all nations he has made, and you will be a people holy to the Lord, your God, as he promised.
text.DRB: and make thee higher than all nations which he created, to his praise, and name, and glory: that thou mayst be a holy people of our Lord thy God, as he hath spoken.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and the elders of Israel charged all the people as follows: Keep the entire commandment that I am commanding you today.
text.Rotherham: And Moses with the elders of Israel commanded the people saying,—Observe all the commandment which I am commanding you to-day.
text.KJV: And Moses with the elders of Israel commanded the people, saying, Keep all the commandments which I command you this day.
text.NABRE: Chapter 27 The Altar on Mount Ebal. Then Moses, with the elders of Israel, commanded the people, saying: Keep this whole commandment which I give you today.
text.DRB: And Moyses and the ancients of Israel commanded the people, saying: Keep every commandment that I command you this day.

text.NRSV: On the day that you cross over the Jordan into the land that the Lord your God is giving you, you shall set up large stones and cover them with plaster.
text.Rotherham: So then it shall be in the day when ye shall pass over the Jordan, into the land which Yahweh thy God is giving unto thee,—that thou shalt rear thee up great stones, and plaster them with plaster;
text.KJV: And it shall be on the day when ye shall pass over Jordan unto the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee, that thou shalt set thee up great stones, and plaister them with plaister:
text.NABRE: On the day you cross the Jordan into the land which the Lord, your God, is giving you, set up some large stones and coat them with plaster.
text.DRB: And when you are passed over Jordan into the land, which our Lord thy God will give thee, thou shalt erect great stones, and shalt polish them with plaster.

text.NRSV: You shall write on them all the words of this law when you have crossed over, to enter the land that the Lord your God is giving you, a land flowing with milk and honey, as the Lord, the God of your ancestors, promised you.
text.Rotherham: and shalt write upon them all the words of this law, when thou shalt pass over,—to the end that thou mayest enter upon the land which Yahweh thy God is giving unto thee a land flowing with milk and honey, as Yahweh the God of thy fathers hath spoken unto thee.
text.KJV: And thou shalt write upon them all the words of this law, when thou art passed over, that thou mayest go in unto the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee, a land that floweth with milk and honey; as the Lord God of thy fathers hath promised thee.
text.NABRE: Write on them, at the time you cross, all the words of this law, so that you may enter the land which the Lord, your God, is giving you, a land flowing with milk and honey, just as the Lord, the God of your ancestors, promised you.
text.DRB: that thou mayst write on them all the words of this law, when thou hast passed over Jordan: that thou mayst enter into the Land, which our Lord thy God will give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, as he sware to thy fathers.

text.NRSV: So when you have crossed over the Jordan, you shall set up these stones, about which I am commanding you today, on Mount Ebal, and you shall cover them with plaster.
text.Rotherham: So then it shall be when ye shall pass over the Jordan, that ye shall rear up these stones which I am commanding you to-day in Mount Ebal,—and thou shalt plaster them with plaster.
text.KJV: Therefore it shall be when ye be gone over Jordan, that ye shall set up these stones, which I command you this day, in mount Ebal, and thou shalt plaister them with plaister.
text.NABRE: When you cross the Jordan, on Mount Ebal you shall set up these stones concerning which I command you today, and coat them with plaster,
text.DRB: When therefore you are passed Jordan, erect the stones, which I command you this day, in mount Hebal, and thou shalt polish them with plaster:

text.NRSV: And you shall build an altar there to the Lord your God, an altar of stones on which you have not used an iron tool.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt build there an altar unto Yahweh thy God,—an altar of stones, thou shalt not wield thereupon any tool of iron.
text.KJV: And there shalt thou build an altar unto the Lord thy God, an altar of stones: thou shalt not lift up any iron tool upon them.
text.NABRE: and you shall build there an altar to the Lord, your God, an altar made of stones that no iron tool has touched.
text.DRB: and thou shalt build there an altar to our Lord thy God of stones, which iron hath not touched,

text.NRSV: You must build the altar of the Lord your God of unhewn stones. Then offer up burnt offerings on it to the Lord your God,
text.Rotherham: Of whole stones, shalt thou build the altar of Yahweh thy God,—then shalt thou cause to go up thereon ascending-sacrifices, unto Yahweh thy God;
text.KJV: Thou shalt build the altar of the Lord thy God of whole stones: and thou shalt offer burnt offerings thereon unto the Lord thy God:
text.NABRE: You shall build this altar of the Lord, your God, with unhewn stones, and shall offer on it burnt offerings to the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: and of stones not fashioned nor polished: and thou shalt put upon it holocausts to our Lord thy God,

text.NRSV: make sacrifices of well-being, and eat them there, rejoicing before the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt sacrifice peace-offerings and shalt eat there,—and rejoice before Yahweh thy God.
text.KJV: And thou shalt offer peace offerings, and shalt eat there, and rejoice before the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: You shall also offer communion sacrifices and eat them there, rejoicing in the presence of the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: and shalt immolate pacific hosts, and eat there, and feast before our Lord thy God.

text.NRSV: You shall write on the stones all the words of this law very clearly.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt write upon the stones all the words of this law doing it plainly and well.
text.KJV: And thou shalt write upon the stones all the words of this law very plainly.
text.NABRE: On the stones you shall inscribe all the words of this law very clearly.
text.DRB: And thou shalt write upon the stones all the words of this law plainly and clearly.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and the levitical priests spoke to all Israel, saying: Keep silence and hear, O Israel! This very day you have become the people of the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And Moses and the priests the Levites spake unto all Israel saying,—Keep silence and hear O Israel, This day, hast thou been made a people unto Yahweh thy God.
text.KJV: And Moses and the priests the Levites spake unto all Israel, saying, Take heed, and hearken, O Israel; this day thou art become the people of the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: Moses, with the levitical priests, then said to all Israel: Be silent, Israel, and listen! This day you have become the people of the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: And Moyses and the Priests of the Levitical stock said to all Israel: Attend, and hear Israel: This day thou art made the people of our Lord thy God:

text.NRSV: Therefore obey the Lord your God, observing his commandments and his statutes that I am commanding you today.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt therefore hearken unto the voice of Yahweh thy God,—and do his commandments and his statutes, which I am commanding thee to-day.
text.KJV: Thou shalt therefore obey the voice of the Lord thy God, and do his commandments and his statutes, which I command thee this day.
text.NABRE: You shall obey the voice of the Lord, your God, and keep his commandments and statutes which I am giving you today.
text.DRB: thou shalt hear his voice, and do the commandments and justices, which I command thee.

text.NRSV: The same day Moses charged the people as follows:
text.Rotherham: And Moses commanded the people on that day, saying:
text.KJV: And Moses charged the people the same day, saying,
text.NABRE: Preparation for Blessings and Curses. That same day Moses commanded the people, saying:
text.DRB: And Moyses commanded the people in that day, saying:

text.NRSV: When you have crossed over the Jordan, these shall stand on Mount Gerizim for the blessing of the people: Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Joseph, and Benjamin.
text.Rotherham: These, shall stand to bless the people upon Mount Gerizim, when ye have passed over the Jordan,—Simeon and Levi and Judah, and Issachar and Joseph and Benjamin;
text.KJV: These shall stand upon mount Gerizim to bless the people, when ye are come over Jordan; Simeon, and Levi, and Judah, and Issachar, and Joseph, and Benjamin:
text.NABRE: When you cross the Jordan, these shall stand on Mount Gerizim to bless the people: Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Joseph and Benjamin.
text.DRB: These shall stand to bless the people, upon mount Garizim, when you are past Jordan: Simeon, Levi, Judas, Issachar, Joseph, & Benjamin.

text.NRSV: And these shall stand on Mount Ebal for the curse: Reuben, Gad, Asher, Zebulun, Dan, and Naphtali.
text.Rotherham: and, these, shall stand by the curse in Mount Ebal,—Reuben Gad and Asher, and Zebulun Dan and Naphtali.
text.KJV: And these shall stand upon mount Ebal to curse; Reuben, Gad, and Asher, and Zebulun, Dan, and Naphtali.
text.NABRE: And these shall stand on Mount Ebal for the curse: Reuben, Gad, Asher, Zebulun, Dan and Naphtali.
text.DRB: And over against them these shall stand to curse on mount Hebal: Ruben, Gad, and Aser, and Zabulon, Dan and Nephthali.

text.NRSV: Then the Levites shall declare in a loud voice to all the Israelites:
text.Rotherham: Then shall the Levites respond and say unto every man of Israel with voice uplifted:—
text.KJV: And the Levites shall speak, and say unto all the men of Israel with a loud voice,
text.NABRE: The Twelve Curses. The Levites shall proclaim in a loud voice to all the Israelites:
text.DRB: And the Levites shall pronounce, and say to all the men of Israel with a high voice:

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who makes an idol or casts an image, anything abhorrent to the Lord, the work of an artisan, and sets it up in secret.” All the people shall respond, saying, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be the man that maketh an image—cut or molten—an abomination unto Yahweh the work of the hands of the craftsman and putteth it in a secret place. And all the people shall respond and say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be the man that maketh any graven or molten image, an abomination unto the Lord, the work of the hands of the craftsman, and putteth it in a secret place. And all the people shall answer and say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who makes a carved or molten idol, an abomination to the Lord, the work of a craftsman’s hands, and sets it up in secret!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be the man that maketh a graven and molten thing, the abomination of our Lord, the work of the hands of artificers, & shall put it in secret, and all the people shall answer, and say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who dishonors father or mother.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that holdeth in light esteem his father or his mother. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that setteth light by his father or his mother. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who dishonors father or mother!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that honoureth not his father and mother. And all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who moves a neighbor’s boundary marker.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that moveth back the boundary of his neighbour. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that removeth his neighbour’s landmark. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who moves a neighbor’s boundary markers!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that removeth his neighbour's bounds. And all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who misleads a blind person on the road.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that causeth the blind to wander in the way. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that maketh the blind to wander out of the way. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who misleads the blind on their way!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that maketh the blind to go amiss in his journey. And all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who deprives the alien, the orphan, and the widow of justice.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that perverteth the right of the sojourner, the fatherless, or the widow. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that perverteth the judgment of the stranger, fatherless, and widow. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who deprives the resident alien, the orphan or the widow of justice!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that perverteth the judgment of the stranger, of the pupil and the widow. And all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who lies with his father’s wife, because he has violated his father’s rights.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that lieth with his father’s wife, because he hath turned aside his father’s coverlet. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that lieth with his father’s wife; because he uncovereth his father’s skirt. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who has relations with his father’s wife, for he dishonors his father’s bed!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that sleepeth with his father's wife, and revealeth the cover of his bed. And all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who lies with any animal.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that lieth with any beast. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that lieth with any manner of beast. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who has relations with any animal!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that lieth with any beast. And all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who lies with his sister, whether the daughter of his father or the daughter of his mother.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that lieth with his sister, daughter of his father or daughter of his mother. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that lieth with his sister, the daughter of his father, or the daughter of his mother. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who has relations with his sister, whether his father’s daughter or his mother’s daughter!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that sleepeth with his sister, the daughter of his father, or of his mother. And all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who lies with his mother-in-law.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that lieth with his mother-in-law. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that lieth with his mother in law. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who has relations with his mother-in-law!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that sleepeth with his mother in law. And all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who strikes down a neighbor in secret.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that smiteth his neighbour secretly. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that smiteth his neighbour secretly. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who strikes down a neighbor in secret!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that secretly striketh his neighbour. And all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who takes a bribe to shed innocent blood.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that taketh a bribe to shed innocent blood. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that taketh reward to slay an innocent person. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone who accepts payment to kill an innocent person!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that taketh gifts, to kill the soul of innocent blood. And all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: “Cursed be anyone who does not uphold the words of this law by observing them.” All the people shall say, “Amen!”
text.Rotherham: Cursed, be he that establisheth not the words of this law to do them. And all the people shall say—Amen.
text.KJV: Cursed be he that confirmeth not all the words of this law to do them. And all the people shall say, Amen.
text.NABRE: “Cursed be anyone whose actions do not uphold the words of this law!” And all the people shall answer, “Amen!”
text.DRB: Cursed be he that abideth not in the words of this law, and fulfilleth them not in work, and all the people shall say: Amen.

text.NRSV: If you will only obey the Lord your God, by diligently observing all his commandments that I am commanding you today, the Lord your God will set you high above all the nations of the earth;
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, if thou wilt, hearken, unto the voice of Yahweh thy God, to observe to do all his commandments which I am commanding thee to-day, then will Yahweh thy God set thee on high, above all the nations of the earth;
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, if thou shalt hearken diligently unto the voice of the Lord thy God, to observe and to do all his commandments which I command thee this day, that the Lord thy God will set thee on high above all nations of the earth:
text.NABRE: Chapter 28 Blessings for Obedience. Now, if you diligently obey the voice of the Lord, your God, carefully observing all his commandments which I give you today, the Lord, your God, will set you high above all the nations of the earth.
text.DRB: But if thou wilt hear the voice of our Lord thy God, that thou do and keep all his commandments, which I command thee this day, our Lord thy God will make thee higher than all nations, that be on the earth.

text.NRSV: all these blessings shall come upon you and overtake you, if you obey the Lord your God:
text.Rotherham: and all these blessings shall come in upon thee, and reach thee,—because thou dost hearken unto the voice of Yahweh thy God:—
text.KJV: And all these blessings shall come on thee, and overtake thee, if thou shalt hearken unto the voice of the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: All these blessings will come upon you and overwhelm you when you obey the voice of the Lord, your God:
text.DRB: And all these blessings shall come upon thee, and overtake thee: yet so if thou hear his precepts.

text.NRSV: Blessed shall you be in the city, and blessed shall you be in the field.
text.Rotherham: Blessed shalt, thou be in the city,—and blessed shalt thou be in the field:
text.KJV: Blessed shalt thou be in the city, and blessed shalt thou be in the field.
text.NABRE: May you be blessed in the city, and blessed in the country!
text.DRB: Blessed shalt thou be in the city, and blessed in the field.

text.NRSV: Blessed shall be the fruit of your womb, the fruit of your ground, and the fruit of your livestock, both the increase of your cattle and the issue of your flock.
text.Rotherham: Blessed shall be the fruit of thy body, and the fruit of thy ground and the fruit of thy cattle,—the young of thy kine, and the ewes of thy flock:
text.KJV: Blessed shall be the fruit of thy body, and the fruit of thy ground, and the fruit of thy cattle, the increase of thy kine, and the flocks of thy sheep.
text.NABRE: Blessed be the fruit of your womb, the produce of your soil and the offspring of your livestock, the issue of your herds and the young of your flocks!
text.DRB: Blessed shall be the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy ground, and the fruit of thy cattle, the troops of thy herds, & the folds of thy sheep.

text.NRSV: Blessed shall be your basket and your kneading bowl.
text.Rotherham: Blessed, shall be thy basket and thy kneading-trough:
text.KJV: Blessed shall be thy basket and thy store.
text.NABRE: Blessed be your grain basket and your kneading bowl!
text.DRB: Blessed shall thy barns be, and blessed thy remains:

text.NRSV: Blessed shall you be when you come in, and blessed shall you be when you go out.
text.Rotherham: Blessed, shalt thou be when thou comest in,—and, blessed, shalt thou be when thou goest out:
text.KJV: Blessed shalt thou be when thou comest in, and blessed shalt thou be when thou goest out.
text.NABRE: May you be blessed in your coming in, and blessed in your going out!
text.DRB: Blessed shalt thou be coming in and going out.

text.NRSV: The Lord will cause your enemies who rise against you to be defeated before you; they shall come out against you one way, and flee before you seven ways.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will deliver thine enemies who rise up against thee, to be routed before thee,—one way, shall they come out against thee, and seven ways, shall they flee before thee:
text.KJV: The Lord shall cause thine enemies that rise up against thee to be smitten before thy face: they shall come out against thee one way, and flee before thee seven ways.
text.NABRE: Victory and Prosperity. The Lord will beat down before you the enemies that rise up against you; they will come out against you from one direction, and flee before you in seven.
text.DRB: Our Lord will give thine enemies, that rise up against thee, to fall down in thy sight: one way they shall come against thee, and seven ways they shall fly from thy face.

text.NRSV: The Lord will command the blessing upon you in your barns, and in all that you undertake; he will bless you in the land that the Lord your God is giving you.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will command to be with thee the blessing, in thy storehouses and in all whereunto thou settest thy hand,—and will bless thee in the land which Yahweh thy God is giving unto thee:
text.KJV: The Lord shall command the blessing upon thee in thy storehouses, and in all that thou settest thine hand unto; and he shall bless thee in the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee.
text.NABRE: The Lord will affirm the blessing upon you, on your barns and on all your undertakings; he will bless you in the land that the Lord, your God, is giving you.
text.DRB: Our Lord will send forth blessing upon thy cellars, and upon all the works of thy hands: and will bless thee in the land, that thou shalt receive.

text.NRSV: The Lord will establish you as his holy people, as he has sworn to you, if you keep the commandments of the Lord your God and walk in his ways.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will confirm thee unto himself for a holy people, as he sware unto thee,—because thou dost keep the commandments of Yahweh thy God, and dost walk in his ways.
text.KJV: The Lord shall establish thee an holy people unto himself, as he hath sworn unto thee, if thou shalt keep the commandments of the Lord thy God, and walk in his ways.
text.NABRE: The Lord will establish you as a holy people, as he swore to you, if you keep the commandments of the Lord, your God, and walk in his ways.
text.DRB: Our Lord will raise thee up unto himself to be a holy people, as he sware to thee: If thou keep the commandments of our Lord thy God, and walk in his ways.

text.NRSV: All the peoples of the earth shall see that you are called by the name of the Lord, and they shall be afraid of you.
text.Rotherham: And all the peoples of the earth shall see that, the name of Yahweh, hath been called upon thee,—and shall be afraid of thee.
text.KJV: And all people of the earth shall see that thou art called by the name of the Lord; and they shall be afraid of thee.
text.NABRE: All the peoples of the earth will see that the name of the Lord is proclaimed over you, and they will be afraid of you.
text.DRB: And all the people of the earth shall see that the name of our Lord is invocated upon thee, and they shall fear thee.

text.NRSV: The Lord will make you abound in prosperity, in the fruit of your womb, in the fruit of your livestock, and in the fruit of your ground in the land that the Lord swore to your ancestors to give you.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh will cause thee to abound in that which is good, in the fruit of thy body, and in the fruit of thy cattle and in the fruit of thy soil,—upon the soil which Yahweh sware unto thy fathers, to give unto thee:
text.KJV: And the Lord shall make thee plenteous in goods, in the fruit of thy body, and in the fruit of thy cattle, and in the fruit of thy ground, in the land which the Lord sware unto thy fathers to give thee.
text.NABRE: The Lord will generously increase the fruit of your womb, the offspring of your livestock, and the produce of your soil, upon the land which the Lord swore to your ancestors he would give you.
text.DRB: Our Lord will make thee abound with all goods, with the fruit of thy womb, & the fruit of thy cattle, with the fruit of thy land, which our Lord sware to thy fathers that he would give thee.

text.NRSV: The Lord will open for you his rich storehouse, the heavens, to give the rain of your land in its season and to bless all your undertakings. You will lend to many nations, but you will not borrow.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will open unto thee his rich storehouse—the heavens, to give the rain of thy land in its season, and to bless every work of thy hand, so shalt thou lend unto many nations, but thou, shalt not borrow:
text.KJV: The Lord shall open unto thee his good treasure, the heaven to give the rain unto thy land in his season, and to bless all the work of thine hand: and thou shalt lend unto many nations, and thou shalt not borrow.
text.NABRE: The Lord will open up for you his rich storehouse, the heavens, to give your land rain in due season and to bless all the works of your hands. You will lend to many nations but borrow from none.
text.DRB: Our Lord will open his most excellent treasure, the heaven, that it may give rain to thy land in due season: and will bless all the works of thy hands. And thou shalt lend to many nations, and thyself shalt take loan of no man.

text.NRSV: The Lord will make you the head, and not the tail; you shall be only at the top, and not at the bottom—if you obey the commandments of the Lord your God, which I am commanding you today, by diligently observing them,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh, will give thee, to be the head, and not the tail, and thou shalt be only above, and shalt not be beneath,—because thou dost hearken unto the commandments of Yahweh thy God, which I am commanding thee, to-day, to observe and to do;
text.KJV: And the Lord shall make thee the head, and not the tail; and thou shalt be above only, and thou shalt not be beneath; if that thou hearken unto the commandments of the Lord thy God, which I command thee this day, to observe and to do them:
text.NABRE: The Lord will make you the head not the tail, the top not the bottom, if you obey the commandments of the Lord, your God, which I am giving you today, observing them carefully,
text.DRB: And our Lord shall make thee the head, and not the tail: and thou shalt be always above, and not under: yet so, if thou wilt hear the commandments of our Lord thy God which I command thee this day, and keep & do them,

text.NRSV: and if you do not turn aside from any of the words that I am commanding you today, either to the right or to the left, following other gods to serve them.
text.Rotherham: and dost not turn side from any of the words which I am commanding you to-day, to the right hand or to the left,—to go after other gods, to serve them,
text.KJV: And thou shalt not go aside from any of the words which I command thee this day, to the right hand, or to the left, to go after other gods to serve them.
text.NABRE: not turning aside, either to the right or to the left, from any of the words which I am giving you today, following other gods and serving them.
text.DRB: & decline not from them, neither to the right hand, nor to the left, nor follow strange gods, nor serve them.

text.NRSV: But if you will not obey the Lord your God by diligently observing all his commandments and decrees, which I am commanding you today, then all these curses shall come upon you and overtake you:
text.Rotherham: But it shall be, if thou do not hearken unto the voice of Yahweh thy God, to observe to do all his commandments and his statutes which I am commanding thee to-day, then shall come in upon thee all these curses and shall reach thee:—
text.KJV: But it shall come to pass, if thou wilt not hearken unto the voice of the Lord thy God, to observe to do all his commandments and his statutes which I command thee this day; that all these curses shall come upon thee, and overtake thee:
text.NABRE: Curses for Disobedience. But if you do not obey the voice of the Lord, your God, carefully observing all his commandments and statutes which I give you today, all these curses shall come upon you and overwhelm you:
text.DRB: But if thou wilt not hear the voice of our Lord thy God, to keep & do all his commandments & ceremonies which I command thee this day, all these curses shall come upon thee, and overtake thee.

text.NRSV: Cursed shall you be in the city, and cursed shall you be in the field.
text.Rotherham: Cursed, shalt thou be in the city,—and cursed, shalt thou be in the field:
text.KJV: Cursed shalt thou be in the city, and cursed shalt thou be in the field.
text.NABRE: May you be cursed in the city, and cursed in the country!
text.DRB: Cursed shalt thou be in the city, cursed in the field.

text.NRSV: Cursed shall be your basket and your kneading bowl.
text.Rotherham: Cursed, shall be thy basket, and thy kneading-trough:
text.KJV: Cursed shall be thy basket and thy store.
text.NABRE: Cursed be your grain basket and your kneading bowl!
text.DRB: Cursed shall thy barn be, and cursed thy remains.

text.NRSV: Cursed shall be the fruit of your womb, the fruit of your ground, the increase of your cattle and the issue of your flock.
text.Rotherham: Cursed, shall be the fruit of thy body, and the fruit of thy ground—the young of thy kine and the ewes of thy flock:
text.KJV: Cursed shall be the fruit of thy body, and the fruit of thy land, the increase of thy kine, and the flocks of thy sheep.
text.NABRE: Cursed be the fruit of your womb, the produce of your soil and the offspring of your livestock, the issue of your herds and the young of your flocks!
text.DRB: Cursed shall be the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy ground, the herds of thy oxen, and the flocks of thy sheep.

text.NRSV: Cursed shall you be when you come in, and cursed shall you be when you go out.
text.Rotherham: Cursed, shalt thou be, when thou comest in,—and, cursed, shalt thou be, when thou goest out:
text.KJV: Cursed shalt thou be when thou comest in, and cursed shalt thou be when thou goest out.
text.NABRE: May you be cursed in your coming in, and cursed in your going out!
text.DRB: Cursed shalt thou be coming in, and cursed going out.

text.NRSV: The Lord will send upon you disaster, panic, and frustration in everything you attempt to do, until you are destroyed and perish quickly, on account of the evil of your deeds, because you have forsaken me.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will send upon thee cursing, confusion and rebuke, in all whereunto thou settest thy hand that thou mayest do it,—until thou be destroyed and until thou perish quickly, because of the wickedness of thy doings, whereby thou hast forsaken me.
text.KJV: The Lord shall send upon thee cursing, vexation, and rebuke, in all that thou settest thine hand unto for to do, until thou be destroyed, and until thou perish quickly; because of the wickedness of thy doings, whereby thou hast forsaken me.
text.NABRE: Sickness and Defeat. The Lord will send on you a curse, panic, and frustration in everything you set your hand to, until you are speedily destroyed and perish for the evil you have done in forsaking me.
text.DRB: Our Lord shall send upon thee famine and hunger, & rebuke upon all the works which thou shalt do: until he consume and destroy thee quickly, for thy most wicked inventions, wherein thou hast forsaken me.

text.NRSV: The Lord will make the pestilence cling to you until it has consumed you off the land that you are entering to possess.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will cause to cleave unto thee—the pestilence,—until he hath consumed thee from off the soil which thou art entering to possess.
text.KJV: The Lord shall make the pestilence cleave unto thee, until he have consumed thee from off the land, whither thou goest to possess it.
text.NABRE: The Lord will make disease cling to you until he has made an end of you from the land you are entering to possess.
text.DRB: Our Lord set the pestilence upon thee, until he consume thee out of the land, which thou shalt enter in to possess.

text.NRSV: The Lord will afflict you with consumption, fever, inflammation, with fiery heat and drought, and with blight and mildew; they shall pursue you until you perish.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, will smite thee, with consumption and with fever, and with inflammation and with violent heat and with the sword, and with blight and with mildew,—and they shall pursue thee, until thou perish.
text.KJV: The Lord shall smite thee with a consumption, and with a fever, and with an inflammation, and with an extreme burning, and with the sword, and with blasting, and with mildew; and they shall pursue thee until thou perish.
text.NABRE: The Lord will strike you with consumption, fever, and inflammation, with fiery heat and drought, with blight and mildew, that will pursue you until you perish.
text.DRB: Our Lord strike thee with poverty, with the fever and cold, with burning and heat, & with corrupt air and blasting, and pursue thee till thou perish.

text.NRSV: The sky over your head shall be bronze, and the earth under you iron.
text.Rotherham: And thy heavens which are over thy head shall become bronze,—and the earth which is under thee iron.
text.KJV: And thy heaven that is over thy head shall be brass, and the earth that is under thee shall be iron.
text.NABRE: The heavens over your heads will be like bronze and the earth under your feet like iron.
text.DRB: Be the heaven that is over thee, of brass, & the ground that thou treadest, of Iron.

text.NRSV: The Lord will change the rain of your land into powder, and only dust shall come down upon you from the sky until you are destroyed.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will cause the rain of thy land to be powder and dust,—out of the heavens, shall it come down upon thee, until thou be destroyed.
text.KJV: The Lord shall make the rain of thy land powder and dust: from heaven shall it come down upon thee, until thou be destroyed.
text.NABRE: For rain the Lord will give your land powdery dust, which will come down upon you from the heavens until you are destroyed.
text.DRB: Our Lord give dust for rain upon thy land, and ashes descend from heaven upon thee, till thou be consumed.

text.NRSV: The Lord will cause you to be defeated before your enemies; you shall go out against them one way and flee before them seven ways. You shall become an object of horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will give thee up to be routed before thine enemies, one way, shalt thou go out against them, and, seven ways, shalt thou flee before them,—and thou shalt become a terror unto all the kingdoms of the earth.
text.KJV: The Lord shall cause thee to be smitten before thine enemies: thou shalt go out one way against them, and flee seven ways before them: and shalt be removed into all the kingdoms of the earth.
text.NABRE: The Lord will let you be beaten down before your enemies; though you advance against them from one direction, you will flee before them in seven, so that you will become an object of horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.
text.DRB: Our Lord deliver thee to fall down before thine enemies. One way go thou forth against them, & fly seven, and be thou dispersed throughout all the Kingdoms of the earth.

text.NRSV: Your corpses shall be food for every bird of the air and animal of the earth, and there shall be no one to frighten them away.
text.Rotherham: And thy dead body shall become food for every bird of the heavens, and for the beast of the earth,—with none to fright them away.
text.KJV: And thy carcase shall be meat unto all fowls of the air, and unto the beasts of the earth, and no man shall fray them away.
text.NABRE: Your corpses will become food for all the birds of the air and for the beasts of the field, with no one to frighten them off.
text.DRB: And be thy carcass meat to all the fowls of the air, & beasts of the earth, and be there none to drive them away.

text.NRSV: The Lord will afflict you with the boils of Egypt, with ulcers, scurvy, and itch, of which you cannot be healed.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, will smite thee, with the burning sores of Egypt and with the hemorrhoids, and with scab, and with itch,—of which thou canst not be healed.
text.KJV: The Lord will smite thee with the botch of Egypt, and with the emerods, and with the scab, and with the itch, whereof thou canst not be healed.
text.NABRE: The Lord will strike you with Egyptian boils and with tumors, skin diseases and the itch, from none of which you can be cured.
text.DRB: Our Lord strike thee with the boyle of Ægypt, and the part of thy body, by the which dung is cast out, with scab also and itch, so that thou canst not be cured.

text.NRSV: The Lord will afflict you with madness, blindness, and confusion of mind;
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, will smite thee, with madness and with blindness,—and with terror of heart;
text.KJV: The Lord shall smite thee with madness, and blindness, and astonishment of heart:
text.NABRE: And the Lord will strike you with madness, blindness and panic,
text.DRB: Our Lord strike thee with madness and blindness and fury of mind,

text.NRSV: you shall grope about at noon as blind people grope in darkness, but you shall be unable to find your way; and you shall be continually abused and robbed, without anyone to help.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt he groping about in noonday brightness, as the blind man gropeth in thick darkness, and thou shalt not make thy ways prosper,—but shalt be only oppressed and spoiled all the days with none to save.
text.KJV: And thou shalt grope at noonday, as the blind gropeth in darkness, and thou shalt not prosper in thy ways: and thou shalt be only oppressed and spoiled evermore, and no man shall save thee.
text.NABRE: so that even at midday you will grope in the dark as though blind, unable to find your way. Despoilment. You will be oppressed and robbed continually, with no one to come to your aid.
text.DRB: & grope thou at midday as the blind is wont to grope in the dark, and direct not thy ways. And at all times sustain thou wrong, and be thou oppressed with violence, neither have thou any to deliver thee.

text.NRSV: You shall become engaged to a woman, but another man shall lie with her. You shall build a house, but not live in it. You shall plant a vineyard, but not enjoy its fruit.
text.Rotherham: A wife, shalt thou betroth, and, another man shall lie with her. A house, shalt thou build, and shalt not dwell therein,—A vineyard, shalt thou plant, and shalt not throw it open;
text.KJV: Thou shalt betroth a wife, and another man shall lie with her: thou shalt build an house, and thou shalt not dwell therein: thou shalt plant a vineyard, and shalt not gather the grapes thereof.
text.NABRE: Though you betroth a wife, another will have her. Though you build a house, you will not live in it. Though you plant a vineyard, you will not pluck its fruits.
text.DRB: Take thou a wife, and another sleep with her. Build thou a house, and dwell not therein. Plant thou a vineyard, and take not the vintage thereof.

text.NRSV: Your ox shall be butchered before your eyes, but you shall not eat of it. Your donkey shall be stolen in front of you, and shall not be restored to you. Your sheep shall be given to your enemies, without anyone to help you.
text.Rotherham: Thine ox slaughtered before thine eyes, and thou shalt not eat thereof, Thine ass stolen from before thee, and shall not be restored to thee,—Thy flock given to thine enemies, and thou shalt have none to save.
text.KJV: Thine ox shall be slain before thine eyes, and thou shalt not eat thereof: thine ass shall be violently taken away from before thy face, and shall not be restored to thee: thy sheep shall be given unto thine enemies, and thou shalt have none to rescue them.
text.NABRE: Your ox will be slaughtered before your eyes, but you will not eat its flesh. Your donkey will be stolen in your presence, but you will never get it back. Your flocks will be given to your enemies, with no one to come to your aid.
text.DRB: Be thy oxe immolated before thee, and thou not eat thereof. Be thy ass taken away in thy sight, and not restored to thee. Be thy sheep given to thine enemies, and be there none to help thee.

text.NRSV: Your sons and daughters shall be given to another people, while you look on; you will strain your eyes looking for them all day but be powerless to do anything.
text.Rotherham: Thy sons and thy daughters given to another people, thine eyes looking on, and failing for them all the day, thine own hand being powerless.
text.KJV: Thy sons and thy daughters shall be given unto another people, and thine eyes shall look, and fail with longing for them all the day long: and there shall be no might in thine hand.
text.NABRE: Your sons and daughters will be given to another people while you strain your eyes looking for them every day, having no power to do anything.
text.DRB: Be thy sons and thy daughters delivered to another people, thine eyes seeing, and dazzling at the sight of them all the day, and be there no strength in thy hand.

text.NRSV: A people whom you do not know shall eat up the fruit of your ground and of all your labors; you shall be continually abused and crushed,
text.Rotherham: The fruit of thy soil, and all thy toilsome produce, shall a people whom thou knowest not, eat up,—and thou shalt be only oppressed and crushed, all the days;
text.KJV: The fruit of thy land, and all thy labours, shall a nation which thou knowest not eat up; and thou shalt be only oppressed and crushed alway:
text.NABRE: A people you do not know will consume the fruit of your soil and of all your labor, and you will be thoroughly oppressed and continually crushed,
text.DRB: The fruits of thy land, and all thy labours let a people eat, which thou knowest not: and be thou always sustaining calumny, and oppressed all days,

text.NRSV: and driven mad by the sight that your eyes shall see.
text.Rotherham: so that thou shalt be mad,—for the sight of thine eyes which thou shalt see.
text.KJV: So that thou shalt be mad for the sight of thine eyes which thou shalt see.
text.NABRE: until you are driven mad by what your eyes must look upon.
text.DRB: and astonished at the terror of those things, which thine eyes shall see.

text.NRSV: The Lord will strike you on the knees and on the legs with grievous boils of which you cannot be healed, from the sole of your foot to the crown of your head.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, will smite thee, with a grievous boil, upon the knees and upon the legs, of which thou canst not be healed,—from the sole of thy foot even unto the crown of thy head.
text.KJV: The Lord shall smite thee in the knees, and in the legs, with a sore botch that cannot be healed, from the sole of thy foot unto the top of thy head.
text.NABRE: The Lord will strike you with malignant boils of which you cannot be cured, on your knees and legs, and from the soles of your feet to the crown of your head.
text.DRB: Our Lord strike thee with a very sore botch in the knees and shanks, and be thou uncurable from the sole of the foot unto the top of thy head.

text.NRSV: The Lord will bring you, and the king whom you set over you, to a nation that neither you nor your ancestors have known, where you shall serve other gods, of wood and stone.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will bring thee and thy king whom thou wilt set up over thee, unto a nation which thou hast not known, thou nor thy fathers,—and thou shalt serve there other gods of wood and of stone.
text.KJV: The Lord shall bring thee, and thy king which thou shalt set over thee, unto a nation which neither thou nor thy fathers have known; and there shalt thou serve other gods, wood and stone.
text.NABRE: Exile. The Lord will bring you, and your king whom you have set over you, to a nation which you and your ancestors have not known, and there you will serve other gods, of wood and stone,
text.DRB: Our Lord shall bring thee, and thy king whom thou shalt appoint over thee, unto a nation, which thou and thy fathers know not: & there thou shalt serve strange gods, wood and stone.

text.NRSV: You shall become an object of horror, a proverb, and a byword among all the peoples where the Lord will lead you.
text.Rotherham: Thus shalt thou become a horror, a byword, and a mockery, among all the peoples whither Yahweh thy God will drive thee.
text.KJV: And thou shalt become an astonishment, a proverb, and a byword, among all nations whither the Lord shall lead thee.
text.NABRE: and you will be a horror, a byword, a taunt among all the peoples to which the Lord will drive you.
text.DRB: And thou shalt be destroyed for a proverb and fable to all peoples, unto whom our Lord shall bring thee in.

text.NRSV: You shall carry much seed into the field but shall gather little in, for the locust shall consume it.
text.Rotherham: Much seed, shalt thou take out into the field,—and little, shalt thou gather in, for the locust shall consume it.
text.KJV: Thou shalt carry much seed out into the field, and shalt gather but little in; for the locust shall consume it.
text.NABRE: Fruitless Labors. Though you take out seed to your field, you will harvest but little, for the locusts will devour it.
text.DRB: Thou shalt cast much seed into the ground, and gather little: because the locusts shall devour all things.

text.NRSV: You shall plant vineyards and dress them, but you shall neither drink the wine nor gather the grapes, for the worm shall eat them.
text.Rotherham: Vineyards, shalt thou plant, and dress,—but wine, shalt thou not drink, neither shalt thou gather the grapes, for the worm shall eat them.
text.KJV: Thou shalt plant vineyards, and dress them, but shalt neither drink of the wine, nor gather the grapes; for the worms shall eat them.
text.NABRE: Though you plant and cultivate vineyards, you will not drink or store up the wine, for the worms will eat them.
text.DRB: Thou shalt plant a vineyard, and dig, and the wine thou shalt not drink, nor gather any thing thereof: because it shall be wasted with worms.

text.NRSV: You shall have olive trees throughout all your territory, but you shall not anoint yourself with the oil, for your olives shall drop off.
text.Rotherham: Olive trees, shalt thou have in all thy bounds,—but with oil, shalt thou not anoint thyself, for thine olives, shall drop off.
text.KJV: Thou shalt have olive trees throughout all thy coasts, but thou shalt not anoint thyself with the oil; for thine olive shall cast his fruit.
text.NABRE: Though you have olive trees throughout your country, you will have no oil for ointment, for your olives will drop off.
text.DRB: Thou shalt have olives in all thy borders, and shalt not be anointed with the oil: because they shall drop away, and perish.

text.NRSV: You shall have sons and daughters, but they shall not remain yours, for they shall go into captivity.
text.Rotherham: Sons and daughters, shalt thou beget, and they shall not be thine, for they shall go into captivity.
text.KJV: Thou shalt beget sons and daughters, but thou shalt not enjoy them; for they shall go into captivity.
text.NABRE: Though you beget sons and daughters, they will not remain with you, for they will go into captivity.
text.DRB: Thou shalt beget sons and daughters, and shalt not enjoy them: because they shall be led into captivity.

text.NRSV: All your trees and the fruit of your ground the cicada shall take over.
text.Rotherham: All thy trees, and the fruit of thy ground, shall the grasshopper, devour.
text.KJV: All thy trees and fruit of thy land shall the locust consume.
text.NABRE: Buzzing insects will take possession of all your trees and the crops of your soil.
text.DRB: All thy trees and the fruits of thy ground the blasting shall consume.

text.NRSV: Aliens residing among you shall ascend above you higher and higher, while you shall descend lower and lower.
text.Rotherham: The sojourner who is in thy midst, shall mount up above thee higher and higher,—whereas, thou, shalt come down lower and lower:
text.KJV: The stranger that is within thee shall get up above thee very high; and thou shalt come down very low.
text.NABRE: The resident aliens among you will rise above you higher and higher, while you sink lower and lower.
text.DRB: The stranger that liveth with thee in the Land, shall ascend over thee, and shall be higher: and thou shalt descend downward, and be inferior.

text.NRSV: They shall lend to you but you shall not lend to them; they shall be the head and you shall be the tail.
text.Rotherham: he, shall lend to thee, but, thou, shalt not lend to him,—he, shall become head, and, thou, shalt become tail.
text.KJV: He shall lend to thee, and thou shalt not lend to him: he shall be the head, and thou shalt be the tail.
text.NABRE: They will lend to you, not you to them. They will become the head, you the tail.
text.DRB: He shall lend thee, and thou shalt not lend him. He shall be as the head, and thou shalt be the tail.

text.NRSV: All these curses shall come upon you, pursuing and overtaking you until you are destroyed, because you did not obey the Lord your God, by observing the commandments and the decrees that he commanded you.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, all these curses, shall come in upon thee, and pursue thee and overtake thee, until thou be destroyed,—because thou didst not hearken unto the voice of Yahweh thy God, to keep his commandments and his statutes which he hath commanded thee;
text.KJV: Moreover all these curses shall come upon thee, and shall pursue thee, and overtake thee, till thou be destroyed; because thou hearkenedst not unto the voice of the Lord thy God, to keep his commandments and his statutes which he commanded thee:
text.NABRE: All these curses will come upon you, pursuing you and overwhelming you, until you are destroyed, because you would not obey the voice of the Lord, your God, by keeping his commandments and statutes which he gave you.
text.DRB: And all these curses shall come upon thee, and pursuing shall overtake thee, till thou perish: because thou heard not the voice of our Lord thy God, nor kept his commandments and ceremonies which he commanded thee.

text.NRSV: They shall be among you and your descendants as a sign and a portent forever.
text.Rotherham: and they shall be upon thee, for a sign, and for a wonder, and upon thy seed, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: And they shall be upon thee for a sign and for a wonder, and upon thy seed for ever.
text.NABRE: They will be a sign and a wonder for you and your descendants for all time.
text.DRB: And they shall be in thee as signs & wonders, and in thy seed for ever:

text.NRSV: Because you did not serve the Lord your God joyfully and with gladness of heart for the abundance of everything,
text.Rotherham: Because thou servedst not Yahweh thy God, with rejoicing, and with gladness of heart, for abundance of all things,
text.KJV: Because thou servedst not the Lord thy God with joyfulness, and with gladness of heart, for the abundance of all things;
text.NABRE: Since you would not serve the Lord, your God, with heartfelt joy for abundance of every kind,
text.DRB: because thou didst not serve our Lord thy God in joy and gladness of heart, for the abundance of all things.

text.NRSV: therefore you shall serve your enemies whom the Lord will send against you, in hunger and thirst, in nakedness and lack of everything. He will put an iron yoke on your neck until he has destroyed you.
text.Rotherham: therefore shalt thou serve thine enemies, whom Yahweh will send against thee, with hunger and with thirst and with nakedness, and with want of all things,—and he, will put a yoke of iron upon thy neck, until he hath destroyed thee.
text.KJV: Therefore shalt thou serve thine enemies which the Lord shall send against thee, in hunger, and in thirst, and in nakedness, and in want of all things: and he shall put a yoke of iron upon thy neck, until he have destroyed thee.
text.NABRE: in hunger and thirst, in nakedness and utter want, you will serve the enemies whom the Lord will send against you. He will put an iron yoke on your neck, until he destroys you.
text.DRB: Thou shalt serve thine enemy, whom our Lord will send upon thee, in hunger, and thirst, and nakedness, and all penury, and he shall put an iron yoke upon thy neck, till he consume thee.

text.NRSV: The Lord will bring a nation from far away, from the end of the earth, to swoop down on you like an eagle, a nation whose language you do not understand,
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will bring against thee a nation from afar, from the end of the earth, as darteth a bird of prey,—a nation whose tongue thou canst not understand;
text.KJV: The Lord shall bring a nation against thee from far, from the end of the earth, as swift as the eagle flieth; a nation whose tongue thou shalt not understand;
text.NABRE: Invasion and Siege. The Lord will raise up against you a nation from afar, from the end of the earth, that swoops down like an eagle, a nation whose language you do not understand,
text.DRB: Our Lord will bring upon thee a Nation from a far, and from the uttermost ends of the earth, in likeness of an eagle that flyeth with vehemency: whose tongue thou canst not understand:

text.NRSV: a grim-faced nation showing no respect to the old or favor to the young.
text.Rotherham: a nation of fierce countenance,—who wilt not respect an elder nor to the young, show favour;
text.KJV: A nation of fierce countenance, which shall not regard the person of the old, nor shew favour to the young:
text.NABRE: a nation of fierce appearance, that shows neither respect for the aged nor mercy for the young.
text.DRB: a very malapert Nation, that will attribute nothing to the ancient, nor have pity on the little one,

text.NRSV: It shall consume the fruit of your livestock and the fruit of your ground until you are destroyed, leaving you neither grain, wine, and oil, nor the increase of your cattle and the issue of your flock, until it has made you perish.
text.Rotherham: then shall he eat the young of thy cattle and the fruit of thy ground until thou art destroyed, who will not leave for thee corn, new wine or oil, the young of thy kine, or the ewes of thy flock,—until he hath caused thee to perish.
text.KJV: And he shall eat the fruit of thy cattle, and the fruit of thy land, until thou be destroyed: which also shall not leave thee either corn, wine, or oil, or the increase of thy kine, or flocks of thy sheep, until he have destroyed thee.
text.NABRE: They will consume the offspring of your livestock and the produce of your soil, until you are destroyed; they will leave you no grain or wine or oil, no issue of herd, no young of flock, until they have brought about your ruin.
text.DRB: and will devour the fruit of thy cattle, and the fruits of thy Land: until thou perish, and will not leave thee wheat, wine, and oil, herds of oxen, and flocks of sheep: until it destroy thee,

text.NRSV: It shall besiege you in all your towns until your high and fortified walls, in which you trusted, come down throughout your land; it shall besiege you in all your towns throughout the land that the Lord your God has given you.
text.Rotherham: And he shall lay siege to thee in all thy gates, until thy high and fortified walls come down wherein thou wast trusting, in all thy land,—yea he will lay siege to thee in all thy gates, in all thy land which Yahweh thy God, hath given, unto thee.
text.KJV: And he shall besiege thee in all thy gates, until thy high and fenced walls come down, wherein thou trustedst, throughout all thy land: and he shall besiege thee in all thy gates throughout all thy land, which the Lord thy God hath given thee.
text.NABRE: They will besiege you in each of your communities, until the great, fortified walls, in which you trust, come tumbling down all over your land. They will besiege you in every community throughout the land which the Lord, your God, has given you,
text.DRB: and consume thee in all thy cities, and thy strong and high walls be destroyed, wherein thou hadst confidence in all thy Land. Thou shalt be besieged within thy gates in all thy Land, which our Lord thy God will give thee:

text.NRSV: In the desperate straits to which the enemy siege reduces you, you will eat the fruit of your womb, the flesh of your own sons and daughters whom the Lord your God has given you.
text.Rotherham: And thou wilt eat the fruit of thy body, the flesh of thy sons and of thy daughters, whom Yahweh thy God hath given unto thee—in the siege and in the straitness wherewith thine enemy will straiten thee.
text.KJV: And thou shalt eat the fruit of thine own body, the flesh of thy sons and of thy daughters, which the Lord thy God hath given thee, in the siege, and in the straitness, wherewith thine enemies shall distress thee:
text.NABRE: and because of the siege and the distress to which your enemy subjects you, you will eat the fruit of your womb, the flesh of your own sons and daughters whom the Lord, your God, has given you.
text.DRB: and thou shalt eat the fruit of thy womb, and the flesh of thy sons & of thy daughters, which our Lord thy God shall give thee, in the distress and vastation wherewith thine enemy shall oppress thee.

text.NRSV: Even the most refined and gentle of men among you will begrudge food to his own brother, to the wife whom he embraces, and to the last of his remaining children,
text.Rotherham: The man that is tender among you, and exceedingly delicate, his eye will be jealous of his brother and of the wife of his bosom, and of the remnant of his sons, whom be might leave behind;
text.KJV: So that the man that is tender among you, and very delicate, his eye shall be evil toward his brother, and toward the wife of his bosom, and toward the remnant of his children which he shall leave:
text.NABRE: The most refined and fastidious man among you will begrudge his brother and his beloved wife and his surviving children,
text.DRB: The man that is delicate in thee & very riotous, shall much envy his own brother, and his wife that lieth in his bosom,

text.NRSV: giving to none of them any of the flesh of his children whom he is eating, because nothing else remains to him, in the desperate straits to which the enemy siege will reduce you in all your towns.
text.Rotherham: so that he will not give to any one of them, of the flesh of his sons which he will eat, because he hath nothing at all left him,—in the siege and in the straitness wherewith thine enemy will straiten thee within all thy gates.
text.KJV: So that he will not give to any of them of the flesh of his children whom he shall eat: because he hath nothing left him in the siege, and in the straitness, wherewith thine enemies shall distress thee in all thy gates.
text.NABRE: any share in the flesh of his children that he himself is using for food because nothing else is left him—such the siege and distress to which your enemy will subject you in all your communities.
text.DRB: so that he shall not give them of the flesh of his children, which he will eat: because he hath nothing else in the siege and penury, wherewith thine enemies shall waste thee within all thy gates.

text.NRSV: She who is the most refined and gentle among you, so gentle and refined that she does not venture to set the sole of her foot on the ground, will begrudge food to the husband whom she embraces, to her own son, and to her own daughter,
text.Rotherham: The tender and delicate woman among you who hath never adventured the sole of her foot to set it upon the ground, through delicateness and through tenderness, her eye shall be jealous of the husband of her bosom, and of her own son, and of her own daughter;
text.KJV: The tender and delicate woman among you, which would not adventure to set the sole of her foot upon the ground for delicateness and tenderness, her eye shall be evil toward the husband of her bosom, and toward her son, and toward her daughter,
text.NABRE: The most fastidious woman among you, who would not venture to set the sole of her foot on the ground, so refined and fastidious is she, will begrudge her beloved husband and her son and daughter
text.DRB: The tender and delicate woman, that could not go upon the ground; nor set down her foot for over-much niceness and tenderness, will envy her husband, that lieth in her bosom, upon the flesh of her son, and daughter:

text.NRSV: begrudging even the afterbirth that comes out from between her thighs, and the children that she bears, because she is eating them in secret for lack of anything else, in the desperate straits to which the enemy siege will reduce you in your towns.
text.Rotherham: both as to her afterbirth that cometh forth from between her feet and as to her children which she shall bear, for she will eat them for want of all things secretly,—in the siege and in the straitness wherewith thine enemy will straiten thee within thine own gates.
text.KJV: And toward her young one that cometh out from between her feet, and toward her children which she shall bear: for she shall eat them for want of all things secretly in the siege and straitness, wherewith thine enemy shall distress thee in thy gates.
text.NABRE: the afterbirth that issues from her womb and the infants she brings forth because she secretly eats them for want of anything else—such the siege and distress to which your enemy will subject you in your communities.
text.DRB: and the filthiness of the after births, that come forth from the midst of her thighs, and upon the children that are born the same hour. For they shall eat them secretly, because of the penury of all things, in the siege and vastation, wherewith thine enemy shall oppress thee within thy gates.

text.NRSV: If you do not diligently observe all the words of this law that are written in this book, fearing this glorious and awesome name, the Lord your God,
text.Rotherham: If thou wilt not take heed to do all the words of this law, which are written in this scroll,—to revere this glorious and reverend name, Yahweh thy God,
text.KJV: If thou wilt not observe to do all the words of this law that are written in this book, that thou mayest fear this glorious and fearful name, THE LORD THY GOD;
text.NABRE: Plagues. If you are not careful to observe all the words of this law which is written in this book, and to fear this glorious and awesome name, the Lord, your God,
text.DRB: Unless thou keep and do all the words of this law, that be written in this volume, and fear his name glorious and terrible, that is, Our Lord thy God:

text.NRSV: then the Lord will overwhelm both you and your offspring with severe and lasting afflictions and grievous and lasting maladies.
text.Rotherham: then will Yahweh make thy plagues wonderful, and the plagues of thy seed,—plagues great and lasting, and diseases grievous and lasting;
text.KJV: Then the Lord will make thy plagues wonderful, and the plagues of thy seed, even great plagues, and of long continuance, and sore sicknesses, and of long continuance.
text.NABRE: the Lord will bring upon you and your descendants wondrous calamities, severe and constant calamities, and malignant and constant sicknesses.
text.DRB: our Lord shall increase thy plagues, and the plagues of thy seed, great plagues and continuing, sore infirmities and perpetual.

text.NRSV: He will bring back upon you all the diseases of Egypt, of which you were in dread, and they shall cling to you.
text.Rotherham: and he will bring back on thee all the sickness of Egypt, because of which thou wast afraid,—and they shall cleave unto thee;
text.KJV: Moreover he will bring upon thee all the diseases of Egypt, which thou wast afraid of; and they shall cleave unto thee.
text.NABRE: He will bring back upon you all the diseases of Egypt which you dread, and they will cling to you.
text.DRB: And he shall turn upon thee all the afflictions of Ægypt, which thou didst fear, and they shall cleave to thee.

text.NRSV: Every other malady and affliction, even though not recorded in the book of this law, the Lord will inflict on you until you are destroyed.
text.Rotherham: even every disease and, every plague, which are not written in this scroll of the law, will Yahweh bring up against thee, until thou art destroyed,
text.KJV: Also every sickness, and every plague, which is not written in the book of this law, them will the Lord bring upon thee, until thou be destroyed.
text.NABRE: Even any sickness or calamity not written in this book of the law, that too the Lord will bring upon you until you are destroyed.
text.DRB: Moreover also all the diseases, and plagues, that be not written in the volume of this law, our Lord will bring upon thee, till he consume thee:

text.NRSV: Although once you were as numerous as the stars in heaven, you shall be left few in number, because you did not obey the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall be left men few in number, whereas ye had become as the stars of the heavens for multitude,—because thou hast not hearkened unto the voice of Yahweh thy God.
text.KJV: And ye shall be left few in number, whereas ye were as the stars of heaven for multitude; because thou wouldest not obey the voice of the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: You who were numerous as the stars of the heavens will be left few in number, because you would not obey the voice of the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: & you shall remain few in number, which before was as the stars of heaven for multitude, because thou heardest not the voice of our Lord thy God.

text.NRSV: And just as the Lord took delight in making you prosperous and numerous, so the Lord will take delight in bringing you to ruin and destruction; you shall be plucked off the land that you are entering to possess.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass that as Yahweh rejoiced over you to do you good and to multiply you, so, will Yahweh rejoice over you, to cause you to perish and to destroy you,—and ye shall be torn away, from off the soil, whither thou art going in to possess it;
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, that as the Lord rejoiced over you to do you good, and to multiply you; so the Lord will rejoice over you to destroy you, and to bring you to nought; and ye shall be plucked from off the land whither thou goest to possess it.
text.NABRE: Exile. Just as the Lord once took delight in making you prosper and grow, so will the Lord now take delight in ruining and destroying you, and you will be plucked out of the land you are now entering to possess.
text.DRB: And as before our Lord rejoiced upon you, doing good to you, & multiplying you: so he shall rejoice destroying and subverting you, so that you may be taken away from the Land, which thou shalt enter to possess.

text.NRSV: The Lord will scatter you among all peoples, from one end of the earth to the other; and there you shall serve other gods, of wood and stone, which neither you nor your ancestors have known.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh will scatter thee among all the peoples, from one end of the earth even unto the other end of the earth,—and thou wilt serve there other gods whom thou hast not known—thou nor thy fathers,—of wood and of stone.
text.KJV: And the Lord shall scatter thee among all people, from the one end of the earth even unto the other; and there thou shalt serve other gods, which neither thou nor thy fathers have known, even wood and stone.
text.NABRE: The Lord will scatter you among all the peoples from one end of the earth to the other, and there you will serve other gods, of wood and stone, which you and your ancestors have not known.
text.DRB: Our Lord shall disperse thee into all peoples, from the farthest parts of the earth to the ends thereof: and there thou shalt serve strange gods, which thou art ignorant of and thy fathers, wood and stone.

text.NRSV: Among those nations you shall find no ease, no resting place for the sole of your foot. There the Lord will give you a trembling heart, failing eyes, and a languishing spirit.
text.Rotherham: And among those nations, shalt thou find no ease, neither shall there be a place of rest for the sole of thy foot,—but Yahweh will give unto thee there a trembling heart, and a failing of eyes and faintness of soul.
text.KJV: And among these nations shalt thou find no ease, neither shall the sole of thy foot have rest: but the Lord shall give thee there a trembling heart, and failing of eyes, and sorrow of mind:
text.NABRE: Among these nations you will find no rest, not even a resting place for the sole of your foot, for there the Lord will give you an anguished heart and wearied eyes and a trembling spirit.
text.DRB: In those nations also thou shalt not be quiet, neither shall there be resting for the step of thy foot. For our Lord will give thee a fearful heart, and dazzling eyes, & a soul consumed with pensiveness:

text.NRSV: Your life shall hang in doubt before you; night and day you shall be in dread, with no assurance of your life.
text.Rotherham: And thy life will be hung up for thee in front,—and thou wilt be in dread by night and by day, and wilt not trust in thy life.
text.KJV: And thy life shall hang in doubt before thee; and thou shalt fear day and night, and shalt have none assurance of thy life:
text.NABRE: Your life will hang in suspense and you will stand in dread both day and night, never sure of your life.
text.DRB: and thy life shall be as it were hanging before thee. Thou shalt fear night and day, and thou shalt not trust in thy life.

text.NRSV: In the morning you shall say, “If only it were evening!” and at evening you shall say, “If only it were morning!” —because of the dread that your heart shall feel and the sights that your eyes shall see.
text.Rotherham: In the morning, thou wilt say—Oh that it were evening! and in the evening, thou wilt say—Oh that it were morning! because of the dread of thy heart which thou wilt dread, and because of the sight of thine eyes which thou wilt see.
text.KJV: In the morning thou shalt say, Would God it were even! and at even thou shalt say, Would God it were morning! for the fear of thine heart wherewith thou shalt fear, and for the sight of thine eyes which thou shalt see.
text.NABRE: In the morning you will say, “Would that it were evening!” and in the evening you will say, “Would that it were morning!” because of the dread that your heart must feel and the sight that your eyes must see.
text.DRB: In the morning thou shalt say: Who will grant me evening? And at evening: Who will grant me morning? For the fearfulness of thy heart, wherewith thou shalt be terrified, and for those things which thou shalt see with thine eyes.

text.NRSV: The Lord will bring you back in ships to Egypt, by a route that I promised you would never see again; and there you shall offer yourselves for sale to your enemies as male and female slaves, but there will be no buyer.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh will take thee back again to Egypt in ships, by the way whereof I said unto thee, Thou shalt not again any more see it. And ye will offer yourselves there for sale unto thine enemies as servants and as handmaids with no one to buy.
text.KJV: And the Lord shall bring thee into Egypt again with ships, by the way whereof I spake unto thee, Thou shalt see it no more again: and there ye shall be sold unto your enemies for bondmen and bondwomen, and no man shall buy you.
text.NABRE: The Lord will send you back in ships to Egypt, by a route which I told you that you would never see again; and there you will offer yourselves for sale to your enemies as male and female slaves, but there will be no buyer.
text.DRB: Our Lord shall bring thee again with ships into Ægypt by the way, whereof he said to thee that thou shouldst see it no more. There shalt thou be sold to thine enemies for bondmen and bondwomen, and no man shall buy you.

text.NRSV: These are the words of the covenant that the Lord commanded Moses to make with the Israelites in the land of Moab, in addition to the covenant that he had made with them at Horeb.
text.Rotherham: These, are the words of the covenant which Yahweh commanded Moses to solemnise with the sons of Israel in the land of Moab,—besides the covenant, which he solemnised with them in Horeb.
text.KJV: These are the words of the covenant, which the Lord commanded Moses to make with the children of Israel in the land of Moab, beside the covenant which he made with them in Horeb.
text.NABRE: III. Third Address Chapter 29 Past Favors Recalled. Moses summoned all Israel and said to them, You have seen with your own eyes all that the Lord did in the land of Egypt to Pharaoh and all his servants and to all his land,
text.DRB: These are the words of the covenant which our Lord commanded Moyses to make with the children of Israel in the Land of Moab: beside that covenant which he made with them in Horeb.

text.NRSV: Moses summoned all Israel and said to them: You have seen all that the Lord did before your eyes in the land of Egypt, to Pharaoh and to all his servants and to all his land,
text.Rotherham: And Moses called unto all Israel and said unto them,—Ye yourselves, saw all that Yahweh did before your eyes in the land of Egypt, unto Pharaoh and unto all his servants and unto all his land:
text.KJV: And Moses called unto all Israel, and said unto them, Ye have seen all that the Lord did before your eyes in the land of Egypt unto Pharaoh, and unto all his servants, and unto all his land;
text.NABRE: the great testings your own eyes have seen, and those great signs and wonders.
text.DRB: And Moyses called all Israel, and said to them: you saw all things, that our Lord did before you in the Land of Ægypt to Pharao, and to all his servants, and to his whole land,

text.NRSV: the great trials that your eyes saw, the signs, and those great wonders.
text.Rotherham: the great provings which thine own eyes saw,—those great signs and wonders:
text.KJV: The great temptations which thine eyes have seen, the signs, and those great miracles:
text.NABRE: But the Lord has not given you a heart to understand, or eyes to see, or ears to hear until this day.
text.DRB: the great tentations, which thine eyes have seen, those mighty signs, and wonders,

text.NRSV: But to this day the Lord has not given you a mind to understand, or eyes to see, or ears to hear.
text.Rotherham: yet hath Yahweh not given onto you a heart to know, or eyes to see or ears to hear,—until this day.
text.KJV: Yet the Lord hath not given you an heart to perceive, and eyes to see, and ears to hear, unto this day.
text.NABRE: I led you for forty years in the wilderness. Your clothes did not fall from you in tatters nor your sandals from your feet;
text.DRB: and our Lord hath not given you a heart to understand, and eyes to see, and ears that can hear, unto this present day.

text.NRSV: I have led you forty years in the wilderness. The clothes on your back have not worn out, and the sandals on your feet have not worn out;
text.Rotherham: I led you, therefore forty years in the desert,—your mantles fell not with age, from off you, even thy sandal, fell not with age from off thy foot:
text.KJV: And I have led you forty years in the wilderness: your clothes are not waxen old upon you, and thy shoe is not waxen old upon thy foot.
text.NABRE: it was not bread that you ate, nor wine or beer that you drank—so that you might know that I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: He hath brought you forty years by the desert: your garments are not worn out, neither are the shoes of your feet consumed with age.

text.NRSV: you have not eaten bread, and you have not drunk wine or strong drink—so that you may know that I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Bread, ye did not eat, and wine and strong drink, ye did not drink,—that ye might know that, I, Yahweh, was your God.
text.KJV: Ye have not eaten bread, neither have ye drunk wine or strong drink: that ye might know that I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: When you came to this place, Sihon, king of Heshbon, and Og, king of Bashan, came out to engage us in battle, but we defeated them
