text.KJV: They shall be burnt with hunger, and devoured with burning heat, and with bitter destruction: I will also send the teeth of beasts upon them, with the poison of serpents of the dust.
text.NABRE: Emaciating hunger and consuming fever and bitter pestilence, And the teeth of wild beasts I will send among them, with the venom of reptiles gliding in the dust.
text.DRB: They shall be consumed with famine, & birds shall devour them with most bitter biting: the teeth of beasts will I send upon them, with the fury of those that trail upon the ground, and creep.

text.NRSV: In the street the sword shall bereave, and in the chambers terror, for young man and woman alike, nursing child and old gray head.
text.Rotherham: Without, shall the sword bereave, In the inmost recesses, terror,—To both young man and virgin, Suckling, with man of grey hairs.
text.KJV: The sword without, and terror within, shall destroy both the young man and the virgin, the suckling also with the man of gray hairs.
text.NABRE: Out in the street the sword shall bereave, and at home the terror For the young man and the young woman alike, the nursing babe as well as the gray beard.
text.DRB: Without shall the sword devour them, and within fearfulness, the young man and the virgin together, the sucking child with the old man.

text.NRSV: I thought to scatter them and blot out the memory of them from humankind;
text.Rotherham: I said, I would puff them away, I would destroy from mortals, their memory;
text.KJV: I said, I would scatter them into corners, I would make the remembrance of them to cease from among men:
text.NABRE: I said: I will make an end of them and blot out their name from human memory,
text.DRB: Isaid: Where are they? I will make their memory to cease from among man.

text.NRSV: but I feared provocation by the enemy, for their adversaries might misunderstand and say, "Our hand is triumphant; it was not the Lord who did all this."
text.Rotherham: Were it not that the taunt of the foe, I feared, Lest their adversaries should mistake,—Lest they should say—Our own hand, is exalted, It is not Yahweh, therefore who hath wrought all this!
text.KJV: Were it not that I feared the wrath of the enemy, lest their adversaries should behave themselves strangely, and lest they should say, Our hand is high, and the Lord hath not done all this.
text.NABRE: Had I not feared the provocation by the enemy, that their foes might misunderstand, And say, “Our own hand won the victory; the Lord had nothing to do with any of it.”
text.DRB: But for the wrath of the enemies I have deferred: lest perhaps their enemies might be proud, & would say: Our mighty hand, and not the Lord, hath done all these things.

text.NRSV: They are a nation void of sense; there is no understanding in them.
text.Rotherham: For a nation of vanished sagacity, they are,—And there is in them no understanding.
text.KJV: For they are a nation void of counsel, neither is there any understanding in them.
text.NABRE: For they are a nation devoid of reason, having no understanding.
text.DRB: Anation without counsel is it, and without wisdom.

text.NRSV: If they were wise, they would understand this; they would discern what the end would be.
text.Rotherham: If they had been wise, they would have understood this,—They would have given heed to their here-after!
text.KJV: O that they were wise, that they understood this, that they would consider their latter end!
text.NABRE: If they had insight they would realize this, they would understand their end:
text.DRB: O that they were wise, and understood, and would provide for their last!

text.NRSV: How could one have routed a thousand, and two put a myriad to flight, unless their Rock had sold them, the Lord had given them up?
text.Rotherham: Oh! how would one, have chased, a thousand! And, two, put, ten thousand to flight,—Were it not that their Rock had sold them, And, Yahweh, had abandoned them;
text.KJV: How should one chase a thousand, and two put ten thousand to flight, except their Rock had sold them, and the Lord had shut them up?
text.NABRE: “How could one rout a thousand, or two put ten thousand to flight, Unless it was because their Rock sold them, the Lord delivered them up?”
text.DRB: How should one pursue a thousand, and two put ten thousand to flight? Was it not therefore, because their God sold them, and our Lord inclosed them?

text.NRSV: Indeed their rock is not like our Rock; our enemies are fools.
text.Rotherham: For, not like our Rock, is their rock, Our enemies themselves, being judges;
text.KJV: For their rock is not as our Rock, even our enemies themselves being judges.
text.NABRE: Indeed, their “rock” is not like our Rock; our enemies are fools.
text.DRB: For our Lord is not as their gods: our enemies also are judges.

text.NRSV: Their vine comes from the vinestock of Sodom, from the vineyards of Gomorrah; their grapes are grapes of poison, their clusters are bitter;
text.Rotherham: For of the vine of Sodom, is their vine, And of the vineyards of Gomorrah,—Their grapes, are grapes of gall, Clusters of bitter things, are theirs:
text.KJV: For their vine is of the vine of Sodom, and of the fields of Gomorrah: their grapes are grapes of gall, their clusters are bitter:
text.NABRE: For their vine is from the vine of Sodom, from the vineyards of Gomorrah. Their grapes are grapes of poison, and their clusters are bitter.
text.DRB: Of the vineyard of Sodom, is their vineyard, and of the suburbs of Gomorrha: their grape the grape of gall, and the clusters most bitter.

text.NRSV: their wine is the poison of serpents, the cruel venom of asps.
text.Rotherham: The poison of large serpents, is their wine,—Yea the fierce venom of asps.
text.KJV: Their wine is the poison of dragons, and the cruel venom of asps.
text.NABRE: Their wine is the venom of serpents, the cruel poison of vipers.
text.DRB: The gall of dragons their wine, and the venom of Asps, uncurable.

text.NRSV: Is not this laid up in store with me, sealed up in my treasuries?
text.Rotherham: Is not, that, stored up with me,—Sealed up amongst my treasures;
text.KJV: Is not this laid up in store with me, and sealed up among my treasures?
text.NABRE: Is not this stored up with me, sealed up in my storehouses?
text.DRB: Are not these things laid up with me, and signed in my treasures?

text.NRSV: Vengeance is mine, and recompense, for the time when their foot shall slip; because the day of their calamity is at hand, their doom comes swiftly.
text.Rotherham: Unto the days of vengeance and requital: Unto the time their foot shall totter? For, near, is the day of their fate, And their destiny speedeth on.
text.KJV: To me belongeth vengeance, and recompence; their foot shall slide in due time: for the day of their calamity is at hand, and the things that shall come upon them make haste.
text.NABRE: Vengeance is mine and recompense, for the time they lose their footing; Because the day of their disaster is at hand and their doom is rushing upon them!
text.DRB: Revenge is mine, and I will repay them in time, that their foot may slide: the day of perdition is at hand, and the times make haste to be present.

text.NRSV: Indeed the Lord will vindicate his people, have compassion on his servants, when he sees that their power is gone, neither bond nor free remaining.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh will vindicate his people, And upon his servants, will have compassion,—When he seeth that strength is exhausted, And there is no one shut up or at large,
text.KJV: For the Lord shall judge his people, and repent himself for his servants, when he seeth that their power is gone, and there is none shut up, or left.
text.NABRE: Surely, the Lord will do justice for his people; on his servants he will have pity. When he sees their strength is gone, and neither bond nor free is left,
text.DRB: Our Lord will judge his people, and will have mercy on his servants: he shall see that their hand is weakened, & the shut-up also have failed, and the residue be consumed.

text.NRSV: Then he will say: Where are their gods, the rock in which they took refuge,
text.Rotherham: Then will he say, Where are their gods, The rock in whom they have trusted;
text.KJV: And he shall say, Where are their gods, their rock in whom they trusted,
text.NABRE: He will say, Where are their gods, the rock in whom they took refuge,
text.DRB: And he shall say: Where are their gods, in whom they had confidence.

text.NRSV: who ate the fat of their sacrifices, and drank the wine of their libations? Let them rise up and help you, let them be your protection!
text.Rotherham: Who used to eat the fat of their sacrifices, To drink the wine of their libations? Let them rise up and help you, Let them be over you for a coveting!
text.KJV: Which did eat the fat of their sacrifices, and drank the wine of their drink offerings? let them rise up and help you, and be your protection.
text.NABRE: Who ate the fat of their sacrifices and drank the wine of their libations? Let them rise up now and help you! Let them be your protection!
text.DRB: Of whose victims they did eat fat, and drank the wine of their libaments: Let them arise, and help you, and protect you in necessity.

text.NRSV: See now that I, even I, am he; there is no god besides me. I kill and I make alive; I wound and I heal; and no one can deliver from my hand.
text.Rotherham: See, now, that, I, I, am, he that is, And there are no gods with me,—I, kill—and make alive, I wound and, I, heal, And there is none who, from my hand, can deliver,
text.KJV: See now that I, even I, am he, and there is no god with me: I kill, and I make alive; I wound, and I heal: neither is there any that can deliver out of my hand.
text.NABRE: See now that I, I alone, am he, and there is no god besides me. It is I who bring both death and life, I who inflict wounds and heal them, and from my hand no one can deliver.
text.DRB: See ye that I am only, and there is no other God besides me: I will kill, and I will make to live: I will strike, and I will heal, and there is none that can deliver out of my hand.

text.NRSV: For I lift up my hand to heaven, and swear: As I live forever,
text.Rotherham: For I lift up unto the heavens my hand,—And say, Living am, I, unto times age-abiding:
text.KJV: For I lift up my hand to heaven, and say, I live for ever.
text.NABRE: For I raise my hand to the heavens and will say: As surely as I live forever,
text.DRB: I will lift up my hand to heaven, and will say: I live for ever.

text.NRSV: when I whet my flashing sword, and my hand takes hold on judgment; I will take vengeance on my adversaries, and will repay those who hate me.
text.Rotherham: If I whet my flashing sword, And my hand take hold on justice, I will return vengeance unto mine adversaries, And them who hate me, will I requite:
text.KJV: If I whet my glittering sword, and mine hand take hold on judgment; I will render vengeance to mine enemies, and will reward them that hate me.
text.NABRE: When I sharpen my flashing sword, and my hand lays hold of judgment, With vengeance I will repay my foes and requite those who hate me.
text.DRB: If I shall whet my sword as the lightning, & my hand take judgment: I will repay vengeance to mine enemies, and them that hate me will I requite.

text.NRSV: I will make my arrows drunk with blood, and my sword shall devour flesh— with the blood of the slain and the captives, from the long-haired enemy.
text.Rotherham: I will make mine arrows drunk with blood, And, my sword, shall devour flesh,—With the blood of the slain and the captive, With the flesh of the chief leader of the foe.
text.KJV: I will make mine arrows drunk with blood, and my sword shall devour flesh; and that with the blood of the slain and of the captives, from the beginning of revenges upon the enemy.
text.NABRE: I will make my arrows drunk with blood, and my sword shall devour flesh— With the blood of the slain and the captured, from the long-haired heads of the enemy.
text.DRB: I will imbrue my arrows with blood, and my sword shall devour flesh, of the blood of the slain and captivity, of the bare head of the enemies.

text.NRSV: Praise, O heavens, his people, worship him, all you gods! For he will avenge the blood of his children, and take vengeance on his adversaries; he will repay those who hate him, and cleanse the land for his people.
text.Rotherham: Shout for joy O ye nations [with] his people, For the blood of his servants, he avengeth,—And, vengeance, he returneth unto his adversaries, And is propitious unto the soil of his people.
text.KJV: Rejoice, O ye nations, with his people: for he will avenge the blood of his servants, and will render vengeance to his adversaries, and will be merciful unto his land, and to his people.
text.NABRE: Exult with him, you heavens, bow to him, all you divine beings! For he will avenge the blood of his servants, take vengeance on his foes; He will requite those who hate him, and purge his people’s land.
text.DRB: You gentiles praise his people, because he will revenge the blood of his servants: and will repay vengeance upon their enemies, and will be propitious to the land of his people.

text.NRSV: Moses came and recited all the words of this song in the hearing of the people, he and Joshua son of Nun.
text.Rotherham: So Moses came in and spake all the words of this Song in the hearing of the people,—he and Hoshea son of Nun.
text.KJV: And Moses came and spake all the words of this song in the ears of the people, he, and Hoshea the son of Nun.
text.NABRE: So Moses, together with Hoshea, son of Nun, went and spoke all the words of this song in the hearing of the people.
text.DRB: Moyses therefore came and spake all the words of this canticle in the ears of the people, he and Josue the son of Nun.

text.NRSV: When Moses had finished reciting all these words to all Israel,
text.Rotherham: And, when Moses made an end of speaking all these words unto all Israel,
text.KJV: And Moses made an end of speaking all these words to all Israel:
text.NABRE: Final Appeal. When Moses had finished speaking all these words to all Israel,
text.DRB: And he finished all these words, speaking to the children of Israel,

text.NRSV: he said to them: "Take to heart all the words that I am giving in witness against you today; give them as a command to your children, so that they may diligently observe all the words of this law.
text.Rotherham: he said unto them—Apply your hearts unto all the words which I am witnessing against you, to-day,—how that ye must command your sons to observe to do all the words of this law;
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Set your hearts unto all the words which I testify among you this day, which ye shall command your children to observe to do, all the words of this law.
text.NABRE: he said to them, Take to heart all the words that I am giving in witness against you today, words you should command your children, that they may observe carefully every word of this law.
text.DRB: & he said to them: Set your hearts on all the words, which I testify to you this day: that you command them to your children to keep and to do, and to fulfill all things of this law that are written:

text.NRSV: This is no trifling matter for you, but rather your very life; through it you may live long in the land that you are crossing over the Jordan to possess."
text.Rotherham: for it is not, too small a thing, for you, for, it, is your life,—and, hereby, shall ye prolong your days upon the soil whereunto ye are, passing over the Jordan to possess it.
text.KJV: For it is not a vain thing for you; because it is your life: and through this thing ye shall prolong your days in the land, whither ye go over Jordan to possess it.
text.NABRE: For this is no trivial matter for you, but rather your very life; by this word you will enjoy a long life on the land you are crossing the Jordan to possess.
text.DRB: for not in vain are they commanded you, but that every one should live in them: which doing you may continue a long time in the Land, which passing over Jordan you enter to possess.

text.NRSV: On that very day the Lord addressed Moses as follows:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, on this selfsame day, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses that selfsame day, saying,
text.NABRE: Moses Looks upon Canaan. On that very day the Lord said to Moses,
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses in the same day, saying:

text.NRSV: "Ascend this mountain of the Abarim, Mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, across from Jericho, and view the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the Israelites for a possession;
text.Rotherham: Get thee up into this mountain of the Abarim, Mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, which is over against Jericho—and behold the land of Canaan which I am giving unto the sons of Israel, for a possession;
text.KJV: Get thee up into this mountain Abarim, unto mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, that is over against Jericho; and behold the land of Canaan, which I give unto the children of Israel for a possession:
text.NABRE: Ascend this mountain of the Abarim, Mount Nebo in the land of Moab facing Jericho, and view the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the Israelites as a possession.
text.DRB: Go up into this mount Abarim, that is to say, of passages, into mount Nebo, which is in the Land of Moab against Jericho: and see the Land of Chanaan, which I will deliver to the children of Israel to obtain, and die thou in the mount.

text.NRSV: you shall die there on the mountain that you ascend and shall be gathered to your kin, as your brother Aaron died on Mount Hor and was gathered to his kin;
text.Rotherham: and die thou in the mount whither thou art going up, and be withdrawn unto thy kinsfolk,—as Aaron thy brother died in Mount Hor, and was withdrawn unto his kinsfolk;
text.KJV: And die in the mount whither thou goest up, and be gathered unto thy people; as Aaron thy brother died in mount Hor, and was gathered unto his people:
text.NABRE: Then you shall die on the mountain you are about to ascend, and shall be gathered to your people, just as your brother Aaron died on Mount Hor and there was gathered to his people,
text.DRB: Which going up unto thou shalt be joined to thy peoples, as Aaron thy brother died in mount Hor, and was laid to his people:

text.NRSV: because both of you broke faith with me among the Israelites at the waters of Meribath-kadesh in the wilderness of Zin, by failing to maintain my holiness among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: for that ye acted unfaithfully against me in the midst of the sons of Israel, regarding the waters of Meribah-Kadesh in the desert of Zin,—for that ye hallowed me not in the midst of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: Because ye trespassed against me among the children of Israel at the waters of Meribah–Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin; because ye sanctified me not in the midst of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: because both of you broke faith with me among the Israelites at the waters of Meribath-kadesh in the wilderness of Zin: you did not manifest my holiness among the Israelites.
text.DRB: because you did prevaricate against me in the midst of the children of Israel at the Waters of contradiction in Cades of the desert of Sin: and you did not sanctify me among the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: Although you may view the land from a distance, you shall not enter it—the land that I am giving to the Israelites."
text.Rotherham: Yet, over against thee, shalt thou behold the land,—but thereinto, shalt thou not enter, into the land which I am giving unto the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: Yet thou shalt see the land before thee; but thou shalt not go thither unto the land which I give the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: You may indeed see the land from a distance, but you shall not enter that land which I am giving to the Israelites.
text.DRB: Over against shalt thou see the Land, and shalt not enter into it, which I will give to the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: This is the blessing with which Moses, the man of God, blessed the Israelites before his death.
text.Rotherham: Now, this, is the blessing wherewith Moses, the man of God, blessed the sons of Israel,—before his death.
text.KJV: And this is the blessing, wherewith Moses the man of God blessed the children of Israel before his death.
text.NABRE: Chapter 33 Blessing upon the Tribes. This is the blessing with which Moses, the man of God, blessed the Israelites before he died.
text.DRB: This is the blessing, wherewith Moyses the man of God blessed the children of Israel before his death.

text.Rotherham: And he said—Yahweh from Sinai, came near,—Yea he dawned, out of Seir, upon them, He shone forth out of Mount Paran, Yea he came out of holy myriads,—Out of his right hand [proceeded] fire to guide them.
text.KJV: And he said, The Lord came from Sinai, and rose up from Seir unto them; he shined forth from mount Paran, and he came with ten thousands of saints: from his right hand went a fiery law for them.
text.NABRE: He said: The Lord came from Sinai and dawned on his people from Seir; he shone forth from Mount Paran. With him were myriads of holy ones; at his right hand advanced the gods.
text.DRB: And he said: Our Lord came from Sinai, and from Seir is he risen to us: he hath appeared from mount Pharan, and with him thousands of Saints. In his right hand a fiery law.

text.NRSV:
Indeed, O favorite among peoples,
	all his holy ones were in your charge;
they marched at your heels,
	accepted direction from you.
text.Rotherham: Yea he loved the tribes, All his holy ones, were in thy hand,—Yea, they, were encamped at thy feet, Each one bare away some of thy words.
text.KJV: Yea, he loved the people; all his saints are in thy hand: and they sat down at thy feet; every one shall receive of thy words.
text.NABRE: Indeed, lover of the peoples, all the holy ones are at your side; They follow at your heels, carry out your decisions.
text.DRB: He hath loved the peoples, all the Saints are in his hand: and they that approach to his feet shall receive of his doctrine.

text.NRSV:
Moses charged us with the law,
	as a possession for the assembly of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: A law, did Moses command us,—A possession, for the convocation of Jacob.
text.KJV: Moses commanded us a law, even the inheritance of the congregation of Jacob.
text.NABRE: Moses charged us with the law, as a possession for the assembly of Jacob.
text.DRB: Moyses commanded us a law, the inheritance of the multitude of Jacob.

text.NRSV:
There arose a king in Jeshurun,
	when the leaders of the people assembled—
	the united tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Thus became he in, Jeshurun, a king,—When the heads of the people gathered them-selves together, As one man, the tribes of Israel. [Reuben] —
text.KJV: And he was king in Jeshurun, when the heads of the people and the tribes of Israel were gathered together.
text.NABRE: A king arose in Jeshurun when the chiefs of the people assembled, and the tribes of Israel united.
text.DRB: He shall be king with the most right, the Princes of the people being assembled with the tribes of Israel.

text.NRSV:
May Reuben live, and not die out,
	even though his numbers are few.
text.Rotherham: Let Reuben live and not die,—Nor his men be easily counted.
text.KJV: Let Reuben live, and not die; and let not his men be few.
text.NABRE: May Reuben live and not die out, but let his numbers be few.
text.DRB: Live Ruben, and die he not, and be he little in number.

text.NRSV:
And this he said of Judah:
O Lord, give heed to Judah,
	and bring him to his people;
strengthen his hands for him,
	and be a help against his adversaries.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is for Judah, when he said, Hear, O Yahweh, the voice of Judah, That unto his own people, thou wouldst bring him home,—Let, his own hands, suffice him, And, a helper from his adversaries, do thou become.
text.KJV: And this is the blessing of Judah: and he said, Hear, Lord, the voice of Judah, and bring him unto his people: let his hands be sufficient for him; and be thou an help to him from his enemies.
text.NABRE: Of Judah he said this: Hear, Lord, the voice of Judah, and bring him to his people. His own hands defend his cause; be a help against his foes.
text.DRB: This is the blessing of Judas: Hear Lord the voice of Judas, and bring him in unto his people: his hands shall fight for him, and he shall be his helper against his adversaries.

text.NRSV: And of Levi he said: Give to Levi your Thummim, and your Urim to your loyal one, whom you tested at Massah, with whom you contended at the waters of Meribah;
text.Rotherham: And of Levi, he said: Let, thy perfections and thy lights, belong unto thy man of lovingkindness,—Whom thou didst put to the proof at Massah, With whom thou didst contend over the waters of Meribah;
text.KJV: And of Levi he said, Let thy Thummim and thy Urim be with thy holy one, whom thou didst prove at Massah, and with whom thou didst strive at the waters of Meribah;
text.NABRE: Of Levi he said: Give to Levi your Thummim, your Urim to your faithful one; Him you tested at Massah, contended against him at the waters of Meribah.
text.DRB: To Levi also he said: Thy perfection, and thy doctrine be to thy holy man, whom thou hast proved in tentation, and judged at the Waters of contradiction.

text.NRSV: who said of his father and mother, "I regard them not"; he ignored his kin, and did not acknowledge his children. For they observed your word, and kept your covenant.
text.Rotherham: Who saith of his father and of his mother, I behold them not, And, his own brethren, regardeth he not, And, his own sons, observeth he not,—Because they watch thy speech, That, thy covenant, they may preserve,;
text.KJV: Who said unto his father and to his mother, I have not seen him; neither did he acknowledge his brethren, nor knew his own children: for they have observed thy word, and kept thy covenant.
text.NABRE: He said of his father and mother, “I have no regard for them”; His brothers he would not acknowledge, and his own children he did not recognize. For they kept your words, and your covenant they upheld.
text.DRB: He that said to his father and to his mother: I know you not; and to his brethren: I know you not: and they knew not their children. These kept thy word, and observed thy covenant,

text.NRSV: They teach Jacob your ordinances, and Israel your law; they place incense before you, and whole burnt offerings on your altar.
text.Rotherham: May teach thy regulations unto Jacob, And thy law unto Israel,—May put incense in thy nostrils, And a whole-offering upon thine altar.
text.KJV: They shall teach Jacob thy judgments, and Israel thy law: they shall put incense before thee, and whole burnt sacrifice upon thine altar.
text.NABRE: They teach your ordinances to Jacob, your law to Israel. They bring incense to your nostrils, and burnt offerings to your altar.
text.DRB: thy judgments, o Jacob, and thy law, o Israel: they shall put incense in thy fury, and holocaust upon thine altar.

text.NRSV: Bless, O Lord, his substance, and accept the work of his hands; crush the loins of his adversaries, of those that hate him, so that they do not rise again.
text.Rotherham: Bless, O Yahweh his substance, And with the work of his hands, wilt thou be well pleased,—Crush thou the loins of them who rise up against him, And of them who hate him, that they rise not again.
text.KJV: Bless, Lord, his substance, and accept the work of his hands: smite through the loins of them that rise against him, and of them that hate him, that they rise not again.
text.NABRE: Bless, Lord, his strength, be pleased with the work of his hands. Crush the loins of his adversaries and of his foes, that they may not rise.
text.DRB: Bless Lord his strength, and receive the works of his hands. Strike the backs of his enemies, and they that hate him, let them not rise up.

text.NRSV: Of Benjamin he said: The beloved of the Lord rests in safety— the High God surrounds him all day long— the beloved rests between his shoulders.
text.Rotherham: Of Benjamin, he said, The beloved of Yahweh, shall settle down securely,—With one to throw a shade over him all the day long, Yea, between his shoulders, hath he found rest.
text.KJV: And of Benjamin he said, The beloved of the Lord shall dwell in safety by him; and the Lord shall cover him all the day long, and he shall dwell between his shoulders.
text.NABRE: Of Benjamin he said: The beloved of the Lord, he abides in safety beside him; He shelters him all day long; the beloved abides at his breast.
text.DRB: And to Benjamin he said: The best beloved of our Lord shall dwell confidently in him: as in a bride-chamber all the day shall he abide, and between his shoulders shall he rest.

text.NRSV: And of Joseph he said: Blessed by the Lord be his land, with the choice gifts of heaven above, and of the deep that lies beneath;
text.Rotherham: And of Joseph, he said, Blessed of Yahweh, be his land,—With the precious things of the heavens with the dew, And with the abyss, couching beneath;
text.KJV: And of Joseph he said, Blessed of the Lord be his land, for the precious things of heaven, for the dew, and for the deep that coucheth beneath,
text.NABRE: Of Joseph he said: Blessed by the Lord is his land with the best of heaven above and of the abyss crouching beneath;
text.DRB: To Joseph also he said: Of the blessing of our Lord be his land, of the fruits of heaven, and the dew, & the depth lying underneath.

text.NRSV: with the choice fruits of the sun, and the rich yield of the months;
text.Rotherham: And with the precious fruits of the sun,—And with the precious yield of the moons;
text.KJV: And for the precious fruits brought forth by the sun, and for the precious things put forth by the moon,
text.NABRE: With the best of the produce of the sun, and the choicest yield of the months;
text.DRB: Of the pomes of the fruits of the sun and moon,

text.NRSV: with the finest produce of the ancient mountains, and the abundance of the everlasting hills;
text.Rotherham: And with the chief of the ancient mountains,—And with the precious things of the age-abiding hills;
text.KJV: And for the chief things of the ancient mountains, and for the precious things of the lasting hills,
text.NABRE: With the finest gifts of the ancient mountains and the best from the everlasting hills;
text.DRB: of the tops of the old mountains, of the pomes of the eternal hills:

text.NRSV: with the choice gifts of the earth and its fullness, and the favor of the one who dwells on Sinai. Let these come on the head of Joseph, on the brow of the prince among his brothers.
text.Rotherham: And with the precious things of the earth and its fulness, And with the goodwill of One who dwelt in a thorn-bush.—Let it come on the head of Joseph, And on the crown of the head of him who was consecrated among his brethren.
text.KJV: And for the precious things of the earth and fulness thereof, and for the good will of him that dwelt in the bush: let the blessing come upon the head of Joseph, and upon the top of the head of him that was separated from his brethren.
text.NABRE: With the best of the earth and its fullness, and the favor of the one who dwells on Sinai. Let these come upon the head of Joseph and upon the brow of the prince among his brothers.
text.DRB: and of the fruits of the earth, and of the fullness thereof. The blessing of him, that appeared in the bush, come upon the head of Joseph, and upon the crown of the Nazarite among his brethren.

text.NRSV: A firstborn bull—majesty is his! His horns are the horns of a wild ox; with them he gores the peoples, driving them to the ends of the earth; such are the myriads of Ephraim, such the thousands of Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: His firstborn ox, be an honour to him. And the horns of a buffalo, be his horns, With them, let him thrust, peoples, All at once, to the ends of the earth,—Yea, [with] these, the myriads of Ephraim, And, [with] those, the thousands of Manasseh.
text.KJV: His glory is like the firstling of his bullock, and his horns are like the horns of unicorns: with them he shall push the people together to the ends of the earth: and they are the ten thousands of Ephraim, and they are the thousands of Manasseh.
text.NABRE: His firstborn bull, majesty is his! His horns are the horns of a wild ox; With them he gores the peoples, attacks the ends of the earth. These are the myriads of Ephraim, and these the thousands of Manasseh.
text.DRB: His beauty as of the first born of an oxe, his horns the horns of an unicorn: in them shall he winnow the Nations even to the ends of the earth. These are the multitudes of Ephraim, and these the thousands of Manasses.

text.NRSV: And of Zebulun he said: Rejoice, Zebulun, in your going out; and Issachar, in your tents.
text.Rotherham: And, of Zebulun, he said, Rejoice, Zebulun in thy going out,—And [thou], Issachar in thy tents:
text.KJV: And of Zebulun he said, Rejoice, Zebulun, in thy going out; and, Issachar, in thy tents.
text.NABRE: Of Zebulun he said: Rejoice, Zebulun, in your expeditions, exult, Issachar, in your tents!
text.DRB: And to Zabulon he said: Rejoice Zabulon in thy going out, and Issachar in thy tabernacles.

text.NRSV: They call peoples to the mountain; there they offer the right sacrifices; for they suck the affluence of the seas and the hidden treasures of the sand.
text.Rotherham: The peoples, unto the mountain, will they call, There, will they offer the sacrifices of righteousness,—For, the abundance of the seas, will they suck, Hidden treasures of the sand.
text.KJV: They shall call the people unto the mountain; there they shall offer sacrifices of righteousness: for they shall suck of the abundance of the seas, and of treasures hid in the sand.
text.NABRE: They invite peoples to the mountain where they offer right sacrifices, Because they suck up the abundance of the seas and the hidden treasures of the sand.
text.DRB: They shall call the peoples to the mountain: there shall they immolate the victims of justice. Who shall suck the inundation of the sea as milk, and the hidden treasures of the sands.

text.NRSV: And of Gad he said: Blessed be the enlargement of Gad! Gad lives like a lion; he tears at arm and scalp.
text.Rotherham: And, of Gad, he said: Blessed, be he that hath made room for Gad,—As a lioness, hath he laid himself down, And hath torn an arm likewise the crown of the head;
text.KJV: And of Gad he said, Blessed be he that enlargeth Gad: he dwelleth as a lion, and teareth the arm with the crown of the head.
text.NABRE: Of Gad he said: Blessed be the one who has made Gad so vast! He lies there like a lion; he tears the arm, the head as well.
text.DRB: And to Gad he said: Blessed be Gad in breadth: as a lion hath he rested, and taken the arm and the top of the head.

text.NRSV: He chose the best for himself, for there a commander's allotment was reserved; he came at the head of the people, he executed the justice of the Lord, and his ordinances for Israel.
text.Rotherham: And hath provided a first part for himself, For, there, a commander’s portion hath been hid,—So he cometh with the heads of the people, The righteousness of Yahweh, he executeth, And his regulations, with Israel.
text.KJV: And he provided the first part for himself, because there, in a portion of the lawgiver, was he seated; and he came with the heads of the people, he executed the justice of the Lord, and his judgments with Israel.
text.NABRE: He saw that the best should be his, for there the commander’s portion was assigned; he came at the head of the people. He carried out the justice of the Lord and his ordinances for Israel.
text.DRB: And he saw his principality, that in his part the doctor was reposed: which was with the princes of the people, and did the justices of our Lord, and his judgment with Israel.

text.NRSV: And of Dan he said: Dan is a lion's whelp that leaps forth from Bashan.
text.Rotherham: And, of Dan, he said, Dan, is a lion’s whelp,—He leapeth out of Bashan.
text.KJV: And of Dan he said, Dan is a lion’s whelp: he shall leap from Bashan.
text.NABRE: Of Dan he said: Dan is a lion’s cub, that springs away from a viper!
text.DRB: To Dan also he said: Dan a lion's whelp, he shall flow largely from Basan.

text.NRSV: And of Naphtali he said: O Naphtali, sated with favor, full of the blessing of the Lord, possess the west and the south.
text.Rotherham: And, of Naphtali, he said, O Naphtali! satisfied with favour, And full with the blessing of Yahweh,—The west and the sunny south, do thou possess.
text.KJV: And of Naphtali he said, O Naphtali, satisfied with favour, and full with the blessing of the Lord: possess thou the west and the south.
text.NABRE: Of Naphtali he said: Naphtali, abounding with favor, filled with the blessing of the Lord, take possession of the west and south.
text.DRB: And to Nephthali he said: Nephthali shall enjoy abundance, and shall be full of the blessings of our Lord: the sea and the south he shall possess.

text.NRSV: And of Asher he said: Most blessed of sons be Asher; may he be the favorite of his brothers, and may he dip his foot in oil.
text.Rotherham: And, of Asher, he said, Most blessed of sons, be Asher,—Let him have the goodwill of his brethren, And let him dip, in oil, his foot:
text.KJV: And of Asher he said, Let Asher be blessed with children; let him be acceptable to his brethren, and let him dip his foot in oil.
text.NABRE: Of Asher he said: Most blessed of sons be Asher! May he be the favorite among his brothers, and may he dip his foot in oil!
text.DRB: To Aser also he said: Blessed be Aser in children, be he acceptable to his brethren, and dip he in oil his foot.

text.NRSV: Your bars are iron and bronze; and as your days, so is your strength.
text.Rotherham: Iron and bronze, be thy sandals, And, as thy days, be thy strength.
text.KJV: Thy shoes shall be iron and brass; and as thy days, so shall thy strength be.
text.NABRE: May the bolts of your gates be iron and bronze; may your strength endure through all your days!
text.DRB: His shoe iron and brass. As the days of thy youth, so also thy old age.

text.NRSV: There is none like God, O Jeshurun, who rides through the heavens to your help, majestic through the skies.
text.Rotherham: There is none like GOD, O Jeshurun,—Riding upon the heavens to thy help,—And for his own majesty, on the skies;
text.KJV: There is none like unto the God of Jeshurun, who rideth upon the heaven in thy help, and in his excellency on the sky.
text.NABRE: There is none like the God of Jeshurun, who rides the heavens in his power, who rides the clouds in his majesty;
text.DRB: There is no other God as the God of the rightest: the mounter of heaven is thy helper. By his magnificence the clouds run hither and thither,

text.NRSV: He subdues the ancient gods, shatters the forces of old; he drove out the enemy before you, and said, "Destroy!"
text.Rotherham: Above, is the God of aforetime, And, beneath, are the Arms of the ages,—So he driveth before thee the foe, And doth say—Destroy!
text.KJV: The eternal God is thy refuge, and underneath are the everlasting arms: and he shall thrust out the enemy from before thee; and shall say, Destroy them.
text.NABRE: The God of old is a refuge; a support are the arms of the Everlasting. He drove the enemy out of your way and he said, “Destroy!”
text.DRB: his habitation is above, and under the everlasting arms: he shall cast out the enemy from thy face, and shall say: Be destroyed.

text.NRSV: So Israel lives in safety, untroubled is Jacob's abode in a land of grain and wine, where the heavens drop down dew.
text.Rotherham: Thus hath Israel settled down securely, Alone, the fountain of Jacob, Within a land both of corn and new wine,—His heaven also droppeth down dew.
text.KJV: Israel then shall dwell in safety alone: the fountain of Jacob shall be upon a land of corn and wine; also his heavens shall drop down dew.
text.NABRE: Israel abides securely, Jacob dwells apart, In a land of grain and wine, where the heavens drip with dew.
text.DRB: Israel shall dwell confidently, and alone. The eye of Jacob in the land of corn and wine, and the heavens shall be misty with dew.

text.NRSV: Happy are you, O Israel! Who is like you, a people saved by the Lord, the shield of your help, and the sword of your triumph! Your enemies shall come fawning to you, and you shall tread on their backs.
text.Rotherham: How happy art thou, O Israel! Who is like unto thee, O people victorious in Yahweh? Thy helpful shield, who also is thy majestic sword,—So that thy foes come cringing unto thee, And, thou, upon their heights, dost march along.
text.KJV: Happy art thou, O Israel: who is like unto thee, O people saved by the Lord, the shield of thy help, and who is the sword of thy excellency! and thine enemies shall be found liars unto thee; and thou shalt tread upon their high places.
text.NABRE: Happy are you, Israel! Who is like you, a people delivered by the Lord, Your help and shield, and the sword of your glory. Your enemies cringe before you; you stride upon their backs.
text.DRB: Blessed art thou Israel: who is like to thee, o people that art saved in our Lord, the shield of thy help, and the sword of thy glory? Thy enemies shall deny thee, and thou shalt tread their necks.

text.NRSV: Then Moses went up from the plains of Moab to Mount Nebo, to the top of Pisgah, which is opposite Jericho, and the Lord showed him the whole land: Gilead as far as Dan,
text.Rotherham: So then Moses went up, from the waste plains of Moab into Mount Nebo, the head of Pisgah, which is over against Jericho,—and Yahweh showed him all the land—even Gilead as far as Dan;
text.KJV: And Moses went up from the plains of Moab unto the mountain of Nebo, to the top of Pisgah, that is over against Jericho. And the Lord shewed him all the land of Gilead, unto Dan,
text.NABRE: IV. The Death of Moses Chapter 34 Then Moses went up from the plains of Moab to Mount Nebo, the peak of Pisgah which faces Jericho, and the Lord showed him all the land—Gilead, and as far as Dan,
text.DRB: Moyses therefore went up from the champion of Moab upon mount Nebo, into the top of Phasga against Jericho: and our Lord shewed him all the land of Galaad as far as Dan,

text.NRSV: all Naphtali, the land of Ephraim and Manasseh, all the land of Judah as far as the Western Sea,
text.Rotherham: and all Naphtali, and the land of Ephraim and Manasseh,—and all the land of Judah, as far as the hinder sea;
text.KJV: And all Naphtali, and the land of Ephraim, and Manasseh, and all the land of Judah, unto the utmost sea,
text.NABRE: all Naphtali, the land of Ephraim and Manasseh, all the land of Judah as far as the Western Sea,
text.DRB: and all Nephthali, and the land of Ephraim and Manasses, and all the Land of Juda unto the utmost sea,

text.NRSV: the Negeb, and the Plain—that is, the valley of Jericho, the city of palm trees—as far as Zoar.
text.Rotherham: and the South,—and the circuit of the valley of Jericho, the city of palm trees as far as Zoar.
text.KJV: And the south, and the plain of the valley of Jericho, the city of palm trees, unto Zoar.
text.NABRE: the Negeb, the plain (the valley of Jericho, the City of Palms), and as far as Zoar.
text.DRB: and the south part, and the breadth of the plain of Jericho, a city of palm-trees as far as Segor.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to him, “This is the land of which I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, ‘I will give it to your descendants’; I have let you see it with your eyes, but you shall not cross over there.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto him—This, is the land which I sware to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, Unto thy seed, will I give it. I have let thee see with thine own eyes, But thither, shalt thou not pass over.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto him, This is the land which I sware unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob, saying, I will give it unto thy seed: I have caused thee to see it with thine eyes, but thou shalt not go over thither.
text.NABRE: The Lord then said to him, This is the land about which I promised on oath to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, “I will give it to your descendants.” I have let you see it with your own eyes, but you shall not cross over.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him: This is the Land, for the which I sware to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, saying: To thy seed will I give it. Thou hast seen it with thine eyes, and shalt not pass over to it.

text.NRSV: Then Moses, the servant of the Lord, died there in the land of Moab, at the Lord’s command.
text.Rotherham: So Moses, the servant of Yahweh, died there, in the land of Moab, at the bidding of Yahweh;
text.KJV: So Moses the servant of the Lord died there in the land of Moab, according to the word of the Lord.
text.NABRE: So there, in the land of Moab, Moses, the servant of the Lord, died as the Lord had said;
text.DRB: And Moyses the servant of our Lord died there, in the land of Moab, our Lord commanding it:

text.NRSV: He was buried in a valley in the land of Moab, opposite Beth-peor, but no one knows his burial place to this day.
text.Rotherham: and he buried him, in a valley in the land of Moab, over against Beth-peor,—but no man hath known his burying-place, until this day.
text.KJV: And he buried him in a valley in the land of Moab, over against Beth–peor: but no man knoweth of his sepulchre unto this day.
text.NABRE: and he was buried in a valley in the land of Moab, opposite Beth-peor; to this day no one knows the place of his burial.
text.DRB: and he buried him in the valley of the Land of Moab against Phogor: and no man hath known his sepulchre until this present day.

text.NRSV: Moses was one hundred twenty years old when he died; his sight was unimpaired and his vigor had not abated.
text.Rotherham: Now, Moses, was a hundred and twenty years old, when he died,—his eye had not dimmed, nor had his freshness fled.
text.KJV: And Moses was an hundred and twenty years old when he died: his eye was not dim, nor his natural force abated.
text.NABRE: Moses was one hundred and twenty years old when he died, yet his eyes were undimmed and his vigor unabated.
text.DRB: Moyses was an hundred and twenty years old when he died: his eye was not dim, neither were his teeth moved.

text.NRSV: The Israelites wept for Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days; then the period of mourning for Moses was ended.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel bewailed Moses in the waste plains of Moab thirty days,—then were completed the wailing-days of the mourning for Moses.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel wept for Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days: so the days of weeping and mourning for Moses were ended.
text.NABRE: The Israelites wept for Moses in the plains of Moab for thirty days, till they had completed the period of grief and mourning for Moses.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel mourned him in the champion country of Moab thirty days: & the days of their mourning that mourned for Moyses were accomplished.

text.NRSV: Joshua son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom, because Moses had laid his hands on him; and the Israelites obeyed him, doing as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: But, Joshua, son of Nun, was full of the spirit of wisdom, for Moses had laid his hands upon him,—so the sons of Israel hearkened unto him, and did, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And Joshua the son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom; for Moses had laid his hands upon him: and the children of Israel hearkened unto him, and did as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: Now Joshua, son of Nun, was filled with the spirit of wisdom, since Moses had laid his hands upon him; and so the Israelites gave him their obedience, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And Josue the son of Nun was replenished with the spirit of wisdom, because Moyses did put his hands upon him. And the children of Israel obeyed him, and did as our Lord commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: Never since has there arisen a prophet in Israel like Moses, whom the Lord knew face to face.
text.Rotherham: And there arose not a prophet any more in Israel, like unto Moses,—whom Yahweh acknowledged, face to face;
text.KJV: And there arose not a prophet since in Israel like unto Moses, whom the Lord knew face to face,
text.NABRE: Since then no prophet has arisen in Israel like Moses, whom the Lord knew face to face,
text.DRB: And there rose no more a Prophet in Israel as Moyses, whom our Lord had known face to face,

text.NRSV: He was unequaled for all the signs and wonders that the Lord sent him to perform in the land of Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his servants and his entire land,
text.Rotherham: by all the signs and the wonders which Yahweh sent him to do in the land of Egypt,—unto Pharaoh and unto all his servants and unto all his land;
text.KJV: In all the signs and the wonders, which the Lord sent him to do in the land of Egypt to Pharaoh, and to all his servants, and to all his land,
text.NABRE: in all the signs and wonders the Lord sent him to perform in the land of Egypt against Pharaoh and all his servants and against all his land,
text.DRB: in all signs and wonders, which he sent by him, to do in the Land of Ægypt to Pharao, and to all his servants, and to his whole Land,

text.NRSV: and for all the mighty deeds and all the terrifying displays of power that Moses performed in the sight of all Israel.
text.Rotherham: and by all the firm hand, and by all the great terror which Moses wrought in the sight of all Israel.
text.KJV: And in all that mighty hand, and in all the great terror which Moses shewed in the sight of all Israel.
text.NABRE: and all the great might and the awesome power that Moses displayed in the sight of all Israel.
text.DRB: and all the strong hand, and great marvels, which Moyses did before all Israel.

12.32: Chapter 13 Penalties for Enticing to Idolatry. Every word that I command you, you shall be careful to observe, neither adding to it nor subtracting from it.

22.30: Chapter 23 A man shall not marry his father’s wife, nor shall he dishonor his father’s bed.

29.29: The hidden things belong to the Lord our God, but the revealed things are for us and for our children forever, to observe all the words of this law.
